91bb00d1.theorem:
theorem factory_growth_doubles {x  : ℝ} (hx  : x > 0)  :
  let growth_rate  : ℝ  := 0.1 -- 10% growth rate
  let multiplier  : ℝ  := 1 + growth_rate -- 1.1 multiplier
  (multiplier ^ x = 2) ↔ -- This is the equation we want to prove
  (∀ initial_output  : ℝ, initial_output > 0 →
    initial_output * (1 + growth_rate) ^ x = 2 * initial_output) := by

    (h_dist  : distance = 30)
    (h_time_down  : time_downstream = 2)
    (h_time_up  : time_upstream = 3)
     : ∃! speed_against_current_4  : ℝ,
      ∃ speed_still speed_current  : ℝ,
      -- Downstream equation
      distance = (speed_still + speed_current) * time_downstream ∧
      -- Upstream equation
      distance = (speed_still - speed_current) * time_upstream ∧
      -- Definition of speed against current
      speed_against_current_4 = speed_still - speed_current ∧
      -- The answer
      speed_against_current_4 = 10 := by

theorem moons_median_is_three  : 
  let moons  : List ℕ  := [0, 0, 1, 2, 2, 4, 5, 15, 16, 23]  -- sorted list of moons
  let n  := moons.length
  n = 10 → -- we have 10 planets
  let middle₁  := moons[4]  -- 5th element (0-based indexing)
  let middle₂  := moons[5]  -- 6th element (0-based indexing)
  middle₁ = 2 →  -- 5th element is 2
  middle₂ = 4 →  -- 6th element is 4
  (middle₁ + middle₂) / 2 = 3  -- median formula := by

e497a8a7.theorem:
theorem tetrahedron_perpendicular_relationships (t  : Tetrahedron)  :
  -- BC parallel to PDF
  ((t.B - t.C) • (t.P - t.D) = 0 ∧ (t.B - t.C) • (t.D - t.F) = 0) ∧
  -- DF perpendicular to PAE
  ((t.D - t.F) • (t.P - t.A) = 0 ∧ (t.D - t.F) • (t.A - t.E) = 0) ∧
  -- PAE perpendicular to ABC
  ((t.P - t.A) • (t.A - t.B) = 0 ∧ (t.P - t.A) • (t.B - t.C) = 0 ∧
   (t.P - t.A) • (t.C - t.A) = 0 ∧
   (t.A - t.E) • (t.A - t.B) = 0 ∧ (t.A - t.E) • (t.B - t.C) = 0 ∧
   (t.A - t.E) • (t.C - t.A) = 0) ∧
  -- PDF not perpendicular to ABC
  ¬((t.P - t.A) • (t.D - t.F) = 0 ∧ (t.A - t.B) • (t.D - t.F) = 0 ∧
    (t.B - t.C) • (t.D - t.F) = 0) := by

  (h₂₂  : ∑ i, ∑ j, b i j + ∑ i, ∑ j, c i j + ∑ i, ∑ j, d i j + ∑ i, ∑ j, e i j = 5 * ∑ i, ∑ j, b i j)

    (h_rad  : dist O B = dist O C)  -- OB and OC are radii
    (h_perp_B  : (O.1 - B.1) * (A.1 - B.1) + (O.2 - B.2) * (A.2 - B.2) = 0)  -- AB ⊥ OB

     : Real.pi / 3 = Real.arccos ((B.1 - A.1) * (C.1 - A.1) + (B.2 - A.2) * (C.2 - A.2) /

    (h2  : ∀ n, n ≥ 1 → a n ≠ 0)                  -- Non-zero terms

    (var_hyp  : ((x₁ - 2)^2 + (x₂ - 2)^2 + (x₃ - 2)^2 + (x₄ - 2)^2 + (x₅ - 2)^2) / 5 = 3)  :
    let new_terms  := [2*x₁ + 1, 2*x₂ + 1, 2*x₃ + 1, 2*x₄ + 1, 2*x₅ + 1, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
    let new_mean  := (new_terms.sum) / 10

    (h_not_parab  : b^2 ≠ 2*p*a) (h_y₀  : y₀ ≠ 0) (h_y₀b  : y₀ ≠ b)  : 
    let y₁  := (b*y₀ - 2*p*a)/(y₀ - b)  -- intersection point M₁
    let y₂  := (2*p*a)/y₀               -- intersection point M₂
    let x  := a

    y₁*y₂ = y*(y₁ + y₂) - 2*p*x := by

ff696d22.theorem:
theorem circle_tangent_projection_fixed_point_2
    (O P  : ℝ × ℝ)  -- Center of circle and internal point
    (R  : ℝ)        -- Radius of circle
    (h_R_pos  : R > 0)
    (h_inside  : dist O P < R)  :
    ∃! Q  : ℝ × ℝ, ∀ (A B X Y  : ℝ × ℝ),
    -- For any circle points A, B
    dist O A = R →
    dist O B = R →
    -- For any perpendicular rays from P
    (A - P) • (B - P) = 0 →
    -- X is projection of P on AB
    (X - P) • (B - A) = 0 →
    -- Y is intersection of tangents at A and B
    (Y - A) • (A - O) = 0 →
    (Y - B) • (B - O) = 0 →
    -- Then XY passes through Q
    ∃ t  : ℝ, Q = (1 - t) • X + t • Y := by

theorem mean_std_dev_shift {n  : ℕ} (x  : Fin n → ℝ) (a  : ℝ) (h  : 0 < n)  :
  let mean (x  : Fin n → ℝ)  := (∑ i, x i) / n
  let std_dev (x  : Fin n → ℝ)  := Real.sqrt ((∑ i, (x i - mean x)^2) / n)
  let x'  := fun i => x i + a
  (mean x', std_dev x') = (mean x + a, std_dev x) := by

theorem variance_of_five_numbers_is_two  :
  let nums  : Fin 5 → ℝ  := fun i => match i with
    | ⟨0, _⟩ => 1
    | ⟨1, _⟩ => 2
    | ⟨2, _⟩ => 3
    | ⟨3, _⟩ => 4
    | ⟨4, _⟩ => 5
  -- if the mean is 3
  ∑ i, nums i = 5 * 3 →
  -- then the variance is 2
  ∑ i, (nums i - 3)^2 / 5 = 2 := by

  (h_pos_a  : a > 0)                     -- a is positive
  (h_pos_b  : b > 0)                     -- b is positive
  (h_a_b  : a > b)                       -- a > b
  (h_b_val  : b = Real.sqrt 5)           -- b = √5
  (h_e  : c/a = 2/3)                     -- eccentricity = 2/3
  (h_rel  : a^2 = b^2 + c^2)  :          -- relation between a, b, c
  let C (x y  : ℝ)  := x^2/9 + y^2/5 = 1  -- ellipse equation
  ∀ y m n  : ℝ,                          -- for all points A(4,y) and B(m,n)
  C m n →                               -- B is on ellipse
  4*m + n*y = 0 →                       -- OA ⊥ OB
  (m - 4)^2 + (n - y)^2 ≥ 21 := by

theorem tangent_line_equation_2  : let f (x  : ℝ)  := 2 * x ^ 2
  let P  : ℝ × ℝ  := (1, 2)
  let tangent_line (x y  : ℝ)  := 4 * x - y - 2
  (∀ x, HasDerivAt f (4 * x) x) ∧  -- derivative is 4x
  f P.1 = P.2 →                    -- point (1,2) lies on curve
  tangent_line P.1 P.2 = 0 ∧       -- tangent line passes through P(1,2)
  ∀ x y, tangent_line x y = 0 →    -- verify all points on tangent line
    y - P.2 = 4 * (x - P.1)        -- satisfy point-slope form := by

  let break_time  : ℝ  := 1  -- total break time (two 30-minute breaks = 1 hour)
  let speed1  : ℝ  := 18    -- speed for first third in km/hr
  let speed2  : ℝ  := 24    -- speed for second third in km/hr
  let speed3  : ℝ  := 30    -- speed for third third in km/hr
  let distance  : ℝ  := (total_time - break_time) * 
    (3 / (1/speed1 + 1/speed2 + 1/speed3)) -- total distance formula

0477f287.theorem:
theorem isosceles_triangle_perimeter_12  : ∀ (a b c  : ℝ),
  -- Assume we have a triangle with positive sides
  0 < a ∧ 0 < b ∧ 0 < c ∧
  -- Assume it's a triangle (triangle inequality)
  a + b > c ∧ b + c > a ∧ a + c > b ∧
  -- Assume two sides are equal (isosceles)
  (a = b ∨ b = c ∨ a = c) ∧
  -- Assume two sides are 5 and 8
  ({a, b, c}  : Set ℝ) = {5, 8, 5} →
  -- Then the perimeter is 18 or 21
  a + b + c = 18 ∨ a + b + c = 21 := by

    (n ≥ 4) →  -- at least 4 students (who scored 90)
    (∃ scores  : Fin n → ℝ,
      -- Four students scored 90
      (∃ (i₁ i₂ i₃ i₄  : Fin n), i₁ ≠ i₂ ∧ i₁ ≠ i₃ ∧ i₁ ≠ i₄ ∧ i₂ ≠ i₃ ∧ i₂ ≠ i₄ ∧ i₃ ≠ i₄ ∧
        scores i₁ = 90 ∧ scores i₂ = 90 ∧ scores i₃ = 90 ∧ scores i₄ = 90) ∧
      -- All scores are at least 70
      (∀ i  : Fin n, scores i ≥ 70) ∧
      -- Mean score is 80
      (∑ i  : Fin n, scores i) / n = 80) →
    n ≥ 8 := by

aab0c5fc.header:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 0
open BigOperators Real Nat Topology Rat
aab0c5fc.theorem:
theorem min_points_needed_is_five  :
  ∀ (orig₁ orig₂ orig₃  : ℝ × ℝ),
  orig₁ ≠ orig₂ → orig₂ ≠ orig₃ → orig₃ ≠ orig₁ →
  -- For any original triangle with no equal angles
  (∃! n  : ℕ, 
    -- There exists a minimal number n such that
    (∀ game  : TriangleColoringGame n,
      -- For any game with n points
      ∃ (p₁ p₂ p₃  : ℝ × ℝ),
      -- There exist three points
      p₁ ∈ game.points ∧ p₂ ∈ game.points ∧ p₃ ∈ game.points ∧
      -- That form a similar triangle
      IsSimilarTriangle p₁ p₂ p₃ orig₁ orig₂ orig₃ ∧
      -- All having the same color
      game.coloring p₁ = game.coloring p₂ ∧ game.coloring p₂ = game.coloring p₃) ∧
    -- And no smaller number works
    ∀ m  : ℕ, m < n →
      ∃ game  : TriangleColoringGame m,
        ∀ (p₁ p₂ p₃  : ℝ × ℝ),
        p₁ ∈ game.points → p₂ ∈ game.points → p₃ ∈ game.points →
        ¬(IsSimilarTriangle p₁ p₂ p₃ orig₁ orig₂ orig₃) ∨
        (¬(game.coloring p₁ = game.coloring p₂ ∧ game.coloring p₂ = game.coloring p₃))) := by

  ∃ (A B C  : ℝ × ℝ × ℝ) (α  : Set (ℝ × ℝ × ℝ)),
  -- A, B, C lie on a line (are collinear)
  (∃ (t₁ t₂  : ℝ), A ≠ B ∧ B ≠ C ∧ A = B + t₁ • (B - A) ∧ B = C + t₂ • (C - B)) ∧
  -- A, B, C are all in plane α
  (A ∈ α ∧ B ∈ α ∧ C ∈ α) ∧
  -- But they don't determine α uniquely
  ∃ (β  : Set (ℝ × ℝ × ℝ)), (A ∈ β ∧ B ∈ β ∧ C ∈ β) ∧ α ≠ β := by

  let B  : ℝ  := Real.pi / 3
  ∀ a c  : ℝ, 
  -- Given conditions
  (0 < a ∧ 0 < c) →  -- positive sides
  (a * c = 2) →      -- from area calculation
  (a ^ 2 + c ^ 2 - 2 * a * c * Real.cos B = b ^ 2) →  -- law of cosines

  a + b + c = 3 + Real.sqrt 3 := by

  (h_total  : total = 900)
  (h_freshmen  : freshmen = 300)
  (h_sophomores  : sophomores = 200)
  (h_seniors  : seniors = 400)
  (h_sample  : sample_size = 45)
  (h_sum  : total = freshmen + sophomores + seniors)  :
  let ratio  : ℚ  := sample_size / total

  let sophomores_sample  : ℕ  := (sophomores * sample_size) / total
  let seniors_sample  : ℕ  := (seniors * sample_size) / total
  freshmen_sample = 15 ∧ sophomores_sample = 10 ∧ seniors_sample = 20 := by

  (h₉  : k = a * b * c / (a + b - c) / (a + c - b) / (b + c - a))

  (h₁₁  : a * b * c = (a + b - c) * (a + c - b) * (b + c - a))
  (h₁₂  : k = a * b * c / (a + b - c) / (a + c - b) / (b + c - a))

  (h₁₄  : a * b * c = (a + b - c) * (a + c - b) * (b + c - a))
  (h₁₅  : k = a * b * c / (a + b - c) / (a + c - b) / (b + c - a))
  (h₁₆  : p = a * b * c / (a + b - c) / (a + c - b) / (b + c - a))
  (h₁₇  : a * b * c = (a + b - c) * (a + c - b) * (b + c - a))
  (h₁₈  : k = a * b * c / (a + b - c) / (a + c - b) / (b + c - a))
  (h₁₉  : p = a * b * c / (a + b - c) / (a + c - b) / (b + c - a))
  (h₂₀  : a * b * c = (a + b - c) * (a + c - b) * (b + c - a))
  (h₂₁  : k = a * b * c / (a + b - c) / (a + c - b) / (b + c - a))

  (dist  : (ℝ × ℝ) → (ℝ × ℝ) → ℝ)  -- distance function
  (h_dist_pos  : ∀ x y, dist x y > 0)  -- distance is positive
  (h_dist_symm  : ∀ x y, dist x y = dist y x)  -- distance is symmetric
  (h_dist_tri  : ∀ x y z, dist x z ≤ dist x y + dist y z)  -- triangle inequality
  (h_dist_self  : ∀ x, dist x x = 0)  -- distance to self is 0
  (max_dist min_dist  : ℝ)  -- maximum and minimum distances
  (h_max  : ∀ i j, dist (points i) (points j) ≤ max_dist)  -- max_dist is maximum
  (h_min  : ∀ i j, min_dist ≤ dist (points i) (points j))  -- min_dist is minimum

371345bb.theorem:
theorem intersection_circle_locus_2 
  (c  : ℝ × ℝ) -- Center of circle
  (r  : ℝ) -- Radius of circle
  (hr  : r > 0) -- Radius is positive
  (A B  : ℝ × ℝ) -- Fixed points A and B
  (A₁ B₁  : ℝ × ℝ) -- Moving points A₁ and B₁
  (hA  : dist A c = r) -- A is on circle
  (hB  : dist B c = r) -- B is on circle
  (hA₁  : dist A₁ c = r) -- A₁ is on circle
  (hB₁  : dist B₁ c = r) -- B₁ is on circle
  (harc  : dist A₁ B₁ = dist A B) -- Constant arc length
  (M  : ℝ × ℝ) -- Intersection point M
  (hM  : ∃ t₁ t₂  : ℝ, 
    M = (c.1 + r * cos t₁, c.2 + r * sin t₁) ∧ 
    M = (c.1 + r * cos t₂, c.2 + r * sin t₂)) -- M is on both lines AA₁ and BB₁
   : ∃ C  : ℝ × ℝ, dist M C = r := by

dd8fa8ab.theorem:
theorem linear_regression_prediction_3
  (n  : ℕ) (xs ys  : Fin n → ℝ) 
  (h_n  : n = 10)
  (h_sum_x  : ∑ i, xs i = 80)
  (h_sum_y  : ∑ i, ys i = 20)
  (h_sum_xy  : ∑ i, xs i * ys i = 184)
  (h_sum_x2  : ∑ i, xs i ^ 2 = 720)  :
  let x_mean  : ℝ  := (∑ i, xs i) / n
  let y_mean  : ℝ  := (∑ i, ys i) / n
  let b_hat  : ℝ  := (∑ i, xs i * ys i - n * x_mean * y_mean) / 
                   (∑ i, xs i ^ 2 - n * x_mean ^ 2)
  let a_hat  : ℝ  := y_mean - b_hat * x_mean
  (b_hat = 0.3 ∧ a_hat = -0.4 ∧ (0.3 * 8 + (-0.4) = 2)) := by

970a7b64.theorem:
theorem geometric_sequence_problem_314
  (a  : ℕ → ℝ)  -- The sequence
  (S  : ℕ → ℝ)  -- The partial sums
  (T  : ℕ → ℝ)  -- The partial sums of {naₙ}
  (h_a1  : a 1 = 1/2)  -- First term is 1/2
  (h_geom  : ∃ q  : ℝ, q > 0 ∧ ∀ n  : ℕ, n > 0 → a (n+1) = a n * q)  -- Geometric sequence
  (h_S  : ∀ n  : ℕ, n > 0 → S n = ∑ i in Finset.range n, a (i+1))  -- Definition of Sₙ
  (h_T  : ∀ n  : ℕ, n > 0 → T n = ∑ i in Finset.range n, (i+1) * a (i+1))  -- Definition of Tₙ
  (h_arith  : 2 * (S 5 + a 5) = (S 3 + a 3) + (S 4 + a 4))  -- Arithmetic sequence condition
   : (∀ n  : ℕ, n > 0 → a n = 1/(2^n)) ∧ 
    (∀ n  : ℕ, n > 0 → T n = 2 - (n+2)/(2^n)) := by

d2a16c4b.theorem:
theorem line_circle_intersection_161  : let circle_center  : ℝ × ℝ  := (-1, 3)
                                 let circle_radius  : ℝ  := 2
                                 let line_eq (x y  : ℝ)  := 3*x - y + 2
                                 -- Distance formula denominator
                                 let denom  : ℝ  := Real.sqrt (3^2 + (-1)^2)
                                 -- Distance from point to line formula
                                 let dist_formula (x₀ y₀  : ℝ)  := |line_eq x₀ y₀| / denom
                                 -- Center point is not on line
                                 let center_not_on_line  := line_eq circle_center.1 circle_center.2 ≠ 0
                                 True → -- The line intersects the circle but not at its center
                                 ∃ d  : ℝ, d = dist_formula circle_center.1 circle_center.2 ∧ 
                                         d < circle_radius ∧ center_not_on_line := by

4c68fdf9.theorem:
theorem perpendicular_line_through_center
    (line1  : (x y  : ℝ) → x + y = 0 → Set ℝ × ℝ)  : -- original line
    let C  : ℝ × ℝ  := (-1, 0) -- center of circle
    let line2  := fun (x y  : ℝ) => x - y + 1 = 0 -- proposed perpendicular line
    let slope1  := -1 -- slope of original line
    let slope2  := 1  -- slope of perpendicular line
    -- Then show the following :
    (∀ x y  : ℝ, line2 x y → (x, y) ≠ C → (y - 0)/(x - (-1)) = slope2) ∧ -- line2 has slope 1

4568aa94.theorem:
theorem speed_limit_and_monitor_coincidence  : 
  let speed_limit_seq  := (fun n => 3 + 4 * n  : ℕ → ℕ)  -- speed limit signs starting from 3 km, every 4 km
  let monitor_seq  := (fun n => 10 + 9 * n  : ℕ → ℕ)    -- speed monitors starting from 10 km, every 9 km
  let first_coincidence  := 19                           -- first coincidence at 19 km
  ∃ second_coincidence  : ℕ, 
    second_coincidence = 55 ∧                           -- second coincidence at 55 km
    (∃ n m  : ℕ, speed_limit_seq n = 55 ∧ monitor_seq m = 55) ∧  -- both occur at 55 km
    (∀ k  : ℕ, k < 55 → k ≠ 19 → 
      (∀ n m  : ℕ, speed_limit_seq n ≠ k ∨ monitor_seq m ≠ k)) -- no other coincidences before 55 km := by

theorem parallel_planes_distance_4  : 
  let plane1_normal  : ℝ × ℝ × ℝ  := (3, -2, 6)
  let plane2_normal  : ℝ × ℝ × ℝ  := (3, -2, 6)
  let plane1_constant  : ℝ  := 15
  let plane2_constant  : ℝ  := 3
  let point_on_plane2  : ℝ × ℝ × ℝ  := (1, 0, 1/2)
  -- Verify these are parallel planes
  (plane1_normal = plane2_normal) →
  -- Calculate the distance
  let distance  := |3 * point_on_plane2.1 - 2 * point_on_plane2.2.1 + 
    6 * point_on_plane2.2.2 - plane1_constant| / Real.sqrt (3^2 + (-2)^2 + 6^2)
  distance = 9/7 := by

  let has_trailing_zeros  : ℕ → Bool  := fun k => k % 1000 = 0
  let sum_divisible_by_9  : ℕ → Bool  := fun k => k % 9 = 0
  let alternating_sum_div_11  : ℕ → Bool  := fun k => k % 11 = 0
  ∀ X Y Z  : ℕ, 
    X < 10 ∧ Y < 10 ∧ Z < 10 →
    factorial_15 = 1307674000000 + X * 100000 + Y * 1000 + Z * 100 →
    X = 14 ∧ Y = 3 ∧ Z = 0 := by

e4f18380.theorem:
theorem equilateral_triangle_point_locus_2
    (A B C O  : ℝ × ℝ)  -- Points in 2D plane
    (A₁ B₁  : ℝ × ℝ)    -- Midpoints of sides
    (M  : ℝ × ℝ)        -- Point we're considering
    (h_equilateral  : -- Triangle ABC is equilateral
      dist A B = dist B C ∧ 
      dist B C = dist C A ∧
      dist C A = dist A B)
    (h_center  : -- O is the center of triangle ABC
      O.1 = (A.1 + B.1 + C.1) / 3 ∧
      O.2 = (A.2 + B.2 + C.2) / 3)
    (h_midpoint_A₁  : -- A₁ is midpoint of CB
      A₁ = ((C.1 + B.1) / 2, (C.2 + B.2) / 2))
    (h_midpoint_B₁  : -- B₁ is midpoint of AC
      B₁ = ((A.1 + C.1) / 2, (A.2 + C.2) / 2))
    (h_M_in_quad  : -- M is inside quadrilateral OA₁CB₁
      ∃ t₁ t₂  : ℝ, t₁ ∈ (Set.Icc 0 1) ∧ t₂ ∈ (Set.Icc 0 1) ∧
        M = (t₁ * O.1 + t₂ * A₁.1, t₁ * O.2 + t₂ * A₁.2))  :
    ∃ (t  : ℝ), t ∈ (Set.Icc 0 1) ∧
      ∃ (P  : ℝ × ℝ), -- P is intersection point
        P = (t * A.1 + (1-t) * B.1, t * A.2 + (1-t) * B.2) ∨
        P = (t * C.1 + (1-t) * O.1, t * C.2 + (1-t) * O.2) := by

    (r r₁  : ℝ) (K T₁ T₂ O O₁ M N  : ℝ × ℝ) -- Points in 2D plane
    (hr  : r > 0) (hr₁  : r₁ > 0) -- Positive radii
    (h_dist  : dist O O₁ = r + r₁) -- External tangency condition
    (h_perp₁  : (K.1 - M.1) * (T₁.1 - O.1) + (K.2 - M.2) * (T₁.2 - O.2) = 0) -- OM ⊥ KT₁
    (h_perp₂  : (K.1 - N.1) * (T₂.1 - O₁.1) + (K.2 - N.2) * (T₂.2 - O₁.2) = 0) -- O₁N ⊥ KT₂
    (h_tangent  : (T₁.1 - T₂.1) * (K.1 - O.1) + (T₁.2 - T₂.2) * (K.2 - O.2) = 0) -- T₁T₂ ⊥ OK
    (h_power₁  : dist K T₁ ^ 2 * (r + r₁) = r * dist K T₂ ^ 2) -- Power of point theorem
    (h_power₂  : dist K T₂ ^ 2 * (r + r₁) = r₁ * dist K T₁ ^ 2) -- Power of point theorem
     : dist O M * dist K T₂ ^ 3 = dist O₁ N * dist K T₁ ^ 3 := by

  let f (x  : ℝ)  := x^2 - 3*x + 2
  let roots  := {x  : ℝ | f x = 0}
  let x₁  := 1 -- First root of original equation
  let x₂  := 2 -- Second root of original equation
  -- For equation pair C : y = x, y = √(x+2)
  let g₁ (x  : ℝ)  := x
  let g₂ (x  : ℝ)  := Real.sqrt (x + 2)
  -- For equation pair D : y = x² - 3x + 2, y = 2
  let h₁ (x  : ℝ)  := x^2 - 3*x + 2
  let h₂ (x  : ℝ)  := 2
  -- For equation pair E : y = sin(x), y = 3x - 4
  let i₁ (x  : ℝ)  := Real.sin x
  let i₂ (x  : ℝ)  := 3*x - 4
  ¬(g₁ x₁ = g₂ x₁ ∧ g₁ x₂ = g₂ x₂) ∧ 
  ¬(h₁ x₁ = h₂ x₁ ∧ h₁ x₂ = h₂ x₂) ∧
  ¬(i₁ x₁ = i₂ x₁ ∧ i₁ x₂ = i₂ x₂) := by

theorem tetrahedron_cross_section_perimeter 
  (A B C D  : ℝ × ℝ × ℝ)  -- Points in 3D space
  (AB CD  : ℝ)           -- Lengths of edges
  (dist  : (ℝ × ℝ × ℝ) → (ℝ × ℝ × ℝ) → ℝ)  -- Distance function
  (h_dist_pos  : ∀ x y, dist x y > 0)         -- Distance is positive
  (h_dist_sym  : ∀ x y, dist x y = dist y x)  -- Distance is symmetric
  (h_dist_refl  : ∀ x, dist x x = 0)          -- Distance to self is zero
  (h_equal_edges  : AB = CD)                   -- Two edges are equal
  (h_non_intersect  : AB > 0)                  -- Edges have positive length
  (P Q R S  : ℝ × ℝ × ℝ)                      -- Intersection points
  (h_parallel  : ∃ v  : ℝ × ℝ × ℝ, 
    dist P Q = dist R S ∧                      -- Parallel sides have same length
    dist P S = dist Q R ∧                      -- Parallel sides have same length
    dist P S = AB)                             -- Perpendicular height equals edge length
   : dist P Q + dist Q R + dist R S + dist S P = 2 * AB := by

  (h3  : α ≠ β) -- The roots are distinct
  (a  : ℕ → ℝ) -- Define sequence a_n
  (h_def  : ∀ n  : ℕ, a n = (α^n - β^n)/(α - β))  : -- Definition of a_n
  ∀ n  : ℕ, a (n + 2) = a (n + 1) + a n := by

  let F  : ℝ × ℝ  := (p/2, 0)  -- focus of parabola
  -- B is on parabola and |BF| = 4
  ∃ B  : ℝ × ℝ, B.2^2 = 2*p*B.1 ∧ dist B F = 4 ∧
  -- AB ⊥ AF
  (B.2 - A.2) * (F.1 - A.1) = -(B.1 - A.1) * (F.2 - A.2) →
  p = 2 ∨ p = 6 := by

284d463a.theorem:
theorem equilateral_perpendicular_locus 
    (A B C M D E F  : ℝ × ℝ)  -- Points in R²
    (is_equilateral  : let dist  := fun (p q  : ℝ × ℝ) => Real.sqrt ((p.1 - q.1)^2 + (p.2 - q.2)^2)
                     dist A B = dist B C ∧ dist B C = dist C A)
    (M_in_triangle  : let area  := fun (p q r  : ℝ × ℝ) => 
                     abs ((p.1 * q.2 + q.1 * r.2 + r.1 * p.2) - 
                        (p.2 * q.1 + q.2 * r.1 + r.2 * p.1)) / 2
                    area A B C > 0 ∧ 
                    area M B C > 0 ∧ 
                    area M C A > 0 ∧ 
                    area M A B > 0)
    (D_perp  : (D.1 - M.1) * (C.1 - B.1) + (D.2 - M.2) * (C.2 - B.2) = 0)  -- D is perpendicular to BC
    (E_perp  : (E.1 - M.1) * (A.1 - C.1) + (E.2 - M.2) * (A.2 - C.2) = 0)  -- E is perpendicular to CA
    (F_perp  : (F.1 - M.1) * (B.1 - A.1) + (F.2 - M.2) * (B.2 - A.2) = 0)  -- F is perpendicular to AB
    (angle_FDE  : let dot  := fun (p q r  : ℝ × ℝ) => (p.1 - r.1) * (q.1 - r.1) + (p.2 - r.2) * (q.2 - r.2)
                 let norm_sq  := fun (p q  : ℝ × ℝ) => (p.1 - q.1)^2 + (p.2 - q.2)^2
                 dot F D E / Real.sqrt (norm_sq F E * norm_sq D E) = 0)  -- FDE is 90°
     : let dot  := fun (p q r  : ℝ × ℝ) => (p.1 - r.1) * (q.1 - r.1) + (p.2 - r.2) * (q.2 - r.2)
      let norm_sq  := fun (p q  : ℝ × ℝ) => (p.1 - q.1)^2 + (p.2 - q.2)^2
      dot M B C / Real.sqrt (norm_sq M B * norm_sq M C) = -Real.sqrt 3 / 2  -- BMC is 150° := by

theorem distance_point_to_plane_3  : 
  let P  : ℝ × ℝ × ℝ  := (1, 2/3, 1/3)
  let plane_normal  : ℝ × ℝ × ℝ  := (1, -1, -1)
  let plane_d  : ℝ  := 1
  -- Distance from point to plane = |ax₁ + by₁ + cz₁ + d|/√(a² + b² + c²)
  let distance  := |plane_normal.1 * P.1 + plane_normal.2.1 * P.2.1 + 
    plane_normal.2.2 * P.2.2 + plane_d| / Real.sqrt (plane_normal.1^2 + 
    plane_normal.2.1^2 + plane_normal.2.2^2)
  distance = Real.sqrt 3 / 3 := by

  (R r  : ℝ) (a b c  : ℝ) 
  (h_pos_R  : R > 0) 
  (h_pos_r  : r > 0)
  (h_pos_a  : a > 0)
  (h_pos_b  : b > 0)
  (h_pos_c  : c > 0)
  (h_triangle  : a + b > c ∧ b + c > a ∧ c + a > b) -- triangle inequality
  (h_area  : let p  := (a + b + c) / 2
            r * p = (a * b * c) / (4 * R))  : -- relationship between area and radii
  let p  := (a + b + c) / 2
  let M  := (p - a) * (p - b) + (p - b) * (p - c) + (p - c) * (p - a)
  3 * R^2 - 4 * R * r - r^2 = 3 * R^2 - M := by

  (O  : ℝ × ℝ) -- Center of circle
  (A B C D  : ℝ × ℝ) -- Points on circle
  (R  : ℝ) -- Radius
  (h_pos  : R > 0) -- Radius is positive
  (h_AB_diam  : dist A B = 2 * R) -- AB is diameter
  (h_O_center  : O = midpoint ℝ A B) -- O is center
  (h_AB_diam_perp  : ∀ P, dist P A = dist P B → P = O) -- AB is unique diameter
  (h_CD_chord  : dist C D = 2 * dist M C) -- CD is a chord
  (h_perp  : (B - A) • (D - C) = 0) -- CD ⊥ AB
  (h_ratio  : dist A M / dist M B = 1/3) -- AM :MB = 1 :3
   : let angle_at_A  := Real.arccos ((dist A C)^2 + (dist A D)^2 - (dist C D)^2)/(2 * dist A C * dist A D)
    let angle_at_B  := Real.arccos ((dist B C)^2 + (dist B D)^2 - (dist C D)^2)/(2 * dist B C * dist B D)

f133083e.theorem:
theorem price_change_over_three_years  :
  -- Given a positive initial price
  ∀ (initial_price  : ℝ), initial_price > 0 →
  -- The final price after three changes equals initial_price * 1.0164
  let year_1  := initial_price * (1 + 0.05)  -- 5% increase
  let year_2  := year_1 * (1 + 0.10)        -- 10% increase
  let final_price  := year_2 * (1 - 0.12)    -- 12% decrease
  -- Then the percentage change is 1.64%
  (final_price - initial_price) / initial_price * 100 = 1.64 := by

theorem line_circle_intersection_36  : let l  : ℝ → ℝ × ℝ  := fun t ↦ 
    (1 + (Real.sqrt 2/2)*t, 2 + (Real.sqrt 2/2)*t)
  let C  : ℝ → ℝ × ℝ  := fun θ ↦ 
    (2 + 2*Real.cos θ, 1 + 2*Real.sin θ)
  let center  : ℝ × ℝ  := (2, 1)  -- center of circle C
  let r  : ℝ  := 2               -- radius of circle C
  -- The distance formula from point (x,y) to line ax + by + c = 0
  let dist_point_to_line  : ℝ × ℝ → ℝ  := fun p ↦ 
    |p.1 - p.2 + 1|/Real.sqrt 2
  ∃ t₁ t₂  : ℝ, t₁ ≠ t₂ ∧ 
    -- Two points on line l intersect circle C
    (l t₁).fst = (C θ).fst ∧ (l t₁).snd = (C θ).snd ∧
    (l t₂).fst = (C θ').fst ∧ (l t₂).snd = (C θ').snd ∧
    dist_point_to_line center > 0 := by

  let digits  := [1,2,3,7,8,9]
  let num1  := 138
  let num2  := 279
  -- num1 and num2 are valid 3-digit numbers formed by given digits
  100 * 1 + 10 * 3 + 8 + (100 * 2 + 10 * 7 + 9) = 417 ∧ 
  -- no other arrangement gives smaller sum
  ∀ d₁ d₂ d₃ d₄ d₅ d₆  : ℕ, 
    d₁ ∈ digits → d₂ ∈ digits → d₃ ∈ digits → 
    d₄ ∈ digits → d₅ ∈ digits → d₆ ∈ digits →
    d₁ ≠ d₂ → d₁ ≠ d₃ → d₁ ≠ d₄ → d₁ ≠ d₅ → d₁ ≠ d₆ →
    d₂ ≠ d₃ → d₂ ≠ d₄ → d₂ ≠ d₅ → d₂ ≠ d₆ →
    d₃ ≠ d₄ → d₃ ≠ d₅ → d₃ ≠ d₆ →
    d₄ ≠ d₅ → d₄ ≠ d₆ →
    d₅ ≠ d₆ →
    100 * d₁ + 10 * d₂ + d₃ + (100 * d₄ + 10 * d₅ + d₆) ≥ 417 := by

  let N  : Set ℝ  := {1, 4}
  let A  := (M ∪ N).Finite ∧ (M ∪ N).ncard = 4 → (M ∩ N).Nonempty
  let B  := (M ∩ N).Nonempty → (M ∪ N).Finite ∧ (M ∪ N).ncard = 4

5d7a1e86.theorem:
theorem triangle_projection_type
    (p  : ProjectionProperties)
    (h1  : p.numIntersections ≤ 3) -- At most three edges intersect
    (h2  : p.numIntersections > 0) -- At least one edge intersects
     : ∃ (t  : TriangleProjectionType), 
      match t with
      | TriangleProjectionType.angle => 
          p.numIntersections = 1 ∧ p.parallelEdges = true
      | TriangleProjectionType.strip => 
          p.numIntersections = 2 ∧ p.parallelEdges = true ∧ p.sameSideIntersections = true
      | TriangleProjectionType.doubleAngleWithContinuation => 
          p.numIntersections = 2 ∧ p.parallelEdges = true ∧ p.sameSideIntersections = false
      | TriangleProjectionType.triangle => 
          p.numIntersections = 3 ∧ p.sameSideIntersections = true
      | TriangleProjectionType.angleWithInfiniteFigure => 
          p.numIntersections = 3 ∧ p.sameSideIntersections = false ∧ p.hasInfiniteRegions = true := by

  let radius  : ℝ  := 2
  ∀ x y  : ℝ, circle_eq x y ↔ ((x - center.1)^2 + (y - center.2)^2 = radius^2) := by

theorem system_sampling_proof  : 
    let a₁  := 40
    let a₂  := 72
    let a₃  := 136
    -- n is the number of students
    let n  := 160
    -- We assume these three numbers are part of an arithmetic sequence of length 5
    -- We need to prove the other two numbers are 8 and 104
    ∀ (seq  : Fin 5 → ℕ), 
    (∀ i j k  : Fin 5, seq j - seq i = seq k - seq j → j - i = k - j) → -- arithmetic sequence property
    seq 1 = a₁ → 
    seq 2 = a₂ → 
    seq 4 = a₃ → 
    seq 0 = 8 ∧ seq 3 = 104 := by

  (∀ x ∈ roots, g x = 0) → -- roots are zeros of g
  (roots.ncard = 3000) → -- g has 3000 roots
  (∃ (abs_vals  : Set ℝ), 
    (∀ x ∈ roots, |x| ∈ abs_vals) ∧ 
    (abs_vals.ncard = 1500)) → -- exactly 1500 distinct absolute values
  ∃ (real_roots  : Set ℝ),
    (real_roots ⊆ roots) ∧ -- real roots are subset of all roots
    (∀ x ∈ real_roots, g x = 0) ∧ -- real roots are zeros
    (real_roots.ncard ≥ 3) := by

    (W  : ℝ) -- Total work to be done
    (W_pos  : W > 0) -- Work is positive
    (p_rate  : ℝ  := W/10) -- p's work rate per day
    (q_rate  : ℝ  := W/6) -- q's work rate per day
     : let initial_work  := p_rate * 2 -- Work done by p in first 2 days
      let remaining_work  := W - initial_work -- Remaining work after 2 days
      let combined_rate  := p_rate + q_rate -- Combined work rate when both work
      let combined_days  := remaining_work / combined_rate -- Days to complete remaining work
      2 + combined_days = 5 := by

ce19c496.theorem:
theorem triangle_parallel_intersection_collinearity
  (A B C  : ℝ × ℝ)  -- Points in the plane
  (P Q  : ℝ × ℝ)    -- Intersection points
  (M  : ℝ × ℝ)      -- Interior point
  (E F  : ℝ × ℝ)    -- Intersection points
  (h_parallel  : (P.2 - Q.2) / (P.1 - Q.1) = (B.2 - C.2) / (B.1 - C.1)) -- PQ parallel to BC
  (h_P_on_AB  : ∃ t  : ℝ, 0 ≤ t ∧ t ≤ 1 ∧ P = (1 - t) • A + t • B) -- P lies on AB
  (h_Q_on_AC  : ∃ t  : ℝ, 0 ≤ t ∧ t ≤ 1 ∧ Q = (1 - t) • A + t • C) -- Q lies on AC
  (h_M_inside  : ∃ t₁ t₂  : ℝ, 0 ≤ t₁ ∧ t₁ ≤ 1 ∧ 0 ≤ t₂ ∧ t₂ ≤ 1 ∧ 
    M = (1 - t₁) • A + t₁ • P ∧ M = (1 - t₂) • A + t₂ • Q) -- M lies inside APQ
  (h_E_on_PQ  : ∃ t  : ℝ, 0 ≤ t ∧ t ≤ 1 ∧ E = (1 - t) • P + t • Q) -- E lies on PQ
  (h_F_on_PQ  : ∃ t  : ℝ, 0 ≤ t ∧ t ≤ 1 ∧ F = (1 - t) • P + t • Q) -- F lies on PQ
  (h_E_on_MB  : ∃ t  : ℝ, 0 ≤ t ∧ E = (1 - t) • M + t • B) -- E lies on MB
  (h_F_on_MC  : ∃ t  : ℝ, 0 ≤ t ∧ F = (1 - t) • M + t • C) -- F lies on MC
   : ∃ N  : ℝ × ℝ, ∃ t  : ℝ, N = (1 - t) • A + t • M := by

theorem min_value_independent_of_a_norm 
  (a b  : ℝ × ℝ)  -- vectors in ℝ²
  (ha_nonzero  : a ≠ (0, 0))
  (hb_nonzero  : b ≠ (0, 0))
  (ha_b_neq  : a ≠ b)
  (orthogonal  : a.1 * b.1 + a.2 * b.2 = 0)  : -- a ⊥ b
  let S₁  := 4 * (norm a) * (norm b) + (norm b)^2  -- Case 1 : 0 dot products
  let S₂  := 2 * (norm a) * (norm b) + (norm a)^2 + 2 * (norm b)^2  -- Case 2 : 1 dot product
  let S₃  := 2 * (norm a)^2 + 3 * (norm b)^2  -- Case 3 : 2 dot products
  let S_min  := min S₁ (min S₂ S₃)
  ∃ (f  : ℝ × ℝ → ℝ), S_min = f b := by

  let a  := b * (1 - 0.2)  -- A's salary is 20% less than B's
  let tax_a  := a * (1 - 0.1)  -- A's salary after tax
  let tax_b  := b * (1 - 0.1)  -- B's salary after tax
  let bonus_a  := tax_a * 0.05  -- A's bonus (5% of after-tax)
  let bonus_b  := tax_b * 0.07  -- B's bonus (7% of after-tax)
  let final_a  := tax_a + bonus_a  -- A's final salary
  let final_b  := tax_b + bonus_b  -- B's final salary
  abs ((final_b - final_a) / final_a - 0.2738) < 0.0001 := by

    (a b c d  : Line) -- The four lines
    (perp_1  : perp a b) -- a ⊥ b
    (perp_2  : perp b c) -- b ⊥ c
    (perp_3  : perp c d) -- c ⊥ d
    (perp_4  : perp d a) -- d ⊥ a
    (par_trans  : ∀ l₁ l₂ l₃  : Line, par l₁ l₂ → par l₂ l₃ → par l₁ l₃) -- Parallel transitivity
    (par_symm  : ∀ l₁ l₂  : Line, par l₁ l₂ → par l₂ l₁) -- Parallel symmetry
    (perp_par  : ∀ l₁ l₂ l₃  : Line, perp l₁ l₂ → perp l₂ l₃ → par l₁ l₃) -- Perpendicular transitivity to parallel
     : par b d ∨ par a c := by

66aafb40.theorem:
theorem contractor_work_completion 
  (initial_workers_low_eff  : ℕ  := 15)
  (initial_workers_high_eff  : ℕ  := 10)
  (efficiency_low  : ℝ  := 1)
  (efficiency_high  : ℝ  := 1.5)
  (initial_days  : ℕ  := 40)
  (completed_fraction  : ℝ  := 1/3)
  (fired_workers_low  : ℕ  := 4)
  (fired_workers_high  : ℕ  := 3)  :
  let remaining_days  : ℕ  := 112
  let daily_rate_before  := initial_workers_low * efficiency_low + 
                           initial_workers_high * efficiency_high
  let total_work  := daily_rate_before * initial_days * (1/completed_fraction)
  let remaining_work  := total_work * (1 - completed_fraction)
  let daily_rate_after  := (initial_workers_low - fired_workers_low) * efficiency_low +
                          (initial_workers_high - fired_workers_high) * efficiency_high
  remaining_days = ⌈remaining_work / daily_rate_after⌉ := by

43ef5d09.theorem:
theorem triangle_ratio_problem_54
  (a b c A B C  : ℝ)  -- sides and angles of triangle
  (h_pos_a  : a > 0) (h_pos_b  : b > 0) (h_pos_c  : c > 0)  -- positive sides
  (h_pos_sinA  : sin A ≠ 0) (h_pos_sinB  : sin B ≠ 0) (h_pos_sinC  : sin C ≠ 0)  -- non-zero sines
  (h_law_sines  : a/sin A = b/sin B ∧ b/sin B = c/sin C)  -- law of sines

      a ∈ S → b ∈ S → c ∈ S → d ∈ S → e ∈ S → f ∈ S → g ∈ S → h ∈ S →
      a ≠ b → c ≠ d → e ≠ f → g ≠ h →
      (a = b ∨ a = c ∨ a = d) ∨ 
      (b = c ∨ b = d ∨ b = e) ∨
      (c = d ∨ c = e ∨ c = f) ∨
      (d = e ∨ d = f ∨ d = g) ∨
      (e = f ∨ e = g ∨ e = h) ∨
      (f = g ∨ f = h) ∨
      (g = h))  : 
  ∀ T  : Finset (Finset (ℝ × ℝ × ℝ)), 
  (∀ t ∈ T, t.card = 4 ∧ t ⊆ S) →
  (∀ t₁ t₂  : Finset (ℝ × ℝ × ℝ), t₁ ∈ T → t₂ ∈ T → t₁ ≠ t₂ →

  -- First equation has no solutions with consecutive even integers
  (∀ x y z  : ℕ, 
    x > 0 ∧ y > 0 ∧ z > 0 →
    Even x ∧ Even y ∧ Even z →
    y = x + 2 ∧ z = x + 4 →
    x + y + z ≠ 100) ∧
  -- Second equation has a solution with consecutive even integers
  (∃ x y z w  : ℕ,
    x > 0 ∧ y > 0 ∧ z > 0 ∧ w > 0 ∧
    Even x ∧ Even y ∧ Even z ∧ Even w ∧
    y = x + 2 ∧ z = x + 4 ∧ w = x + 6 ∧
    x + y + z + w = 100) := by

theorem min_age_diff  : 
  ∀ (a b c d e  : ℕ), 
  a > b ∧ b > c ∧ c > d ∧ d > e → -- ages are strictly decreasing
  let diffs  := [
    a - b, a - c, a - d, a - e,
    b - c, b - d, b - e,
    c - d, c - e,
    d - e
  ]
  -- all differences are different
  (∀ i j, i ≠ j → i < 10 → j < 10 → diffs[i]! ≠ diffs[j]!) →
  -- then the difference between oldest and youngest is at least 11
  a - e ≥ 11 := by

  let squareArea  := 2 * x^2 -- Area of square DFEB
  let smallTriangleArea  := x^2/2 -- Area of triangle FGH

  (h_parallel2  : (E.1 - B.1) * (C.2 - A.2) = (E.2 - B.2) * (C.1 - A.1)) -- E-B parallel to AC
  (h_intersect  : ∃ t  : ℝ, 0 < t ∧ t < 1 ∧

2348bd1d.theorem:
theorem intersection_points_sum_zero  : 
  let f (x y  : ℝ)  := x^2 - (x + y + 4)
  let g (x y  : ℝ)  := y^2 - (y - 15*x + 36)
  let x₁  := 2
  let x₂  := -1 + Real.sqrt 5
  let x₃  := -1 - Real.sqrt 5
  -- x₁, x₂, x₃ are the x-coordinates of intersection points
  (∀ y  : ℝ, f x₁ y = 0 ∧ g x₁ y = 0) ∧  -- x₁ is an intersection point
  (∀ y  : ℝ, f x₂ y = 0 ∧ g x₂ y = 0) ∧  -- x₂ is an intersection point
  (∀ y  : ℝ, f x₃ y = 0 ∧ g x₃ y = 0) ∧  -- x₃ is an intersection point
  -- These are the only intersection points
  (∀ x y  : ℝ, f x y = 0 ∧ g x y = 0 → x = x₁ ∨ x = x₂ ∨ x = x₃) →
  x₁ + x₂ + x₃ = 0 := by

theorem min_div_1125_is_77778000  : 
  -- Assumptions about divisibility by 1125
  77778000 % 1125 = 0 ∧ 
  -- Assumption that it's divisible by 9
  (7 + 7 + 7 + 7 + 8 + 0 + 0 + 0) % 9 = 0 ∧ 
  -- Assumption that it's divisible by 125 (last three digits)
  77778000 % 125 = 0 ∧
  -- Assumption that all digits are valid (7,8,0)
  77778000 ≤ 77778888 ∧
  -- Statement that it's the minimum such number
  ∀ n  : ℕ, n < 77778000 → ¬(
    -- Properties of any smaller number
    n % 1125 = 0 ∧
    n % 125 = 0 ∧
    (n.digits 10).all (fun d => d = 7 ∨ d = 8 ∨ d = 0)
  ) := by

set_option maxHeartbeats 0

f556d081.theorem:
theorem triangle_area_calc_53 (A B C  : ℝ) (a b c  : ℝ)  :
  -- Basic assumptions about the triangle
  0 < a ∧ 0 < b ∧ 0 < c →
  -- Side a equals 1
  a = 1 →
  -- Triangle angle sum is π
  A + B + C = Real.pi →
  -- Angle A is π/3
  A = Real.pi / 3 →
  -- Law of sines
  a / Real.sin A = b / Real.sin B →
  b / Real.sin B = c / Real.sin C →
  -- Given condition
  Real.sin A + Real.sin (B - C) = 2 * Real.sin (2 * C) →
  -- Triangle inequality
  a < b + c ∧ b < a + c ∧ c < a + b →
  -- Area formula
  let area  := (1/2) * b * c * Real.sin A
  area = Real.sqrt 3 / 6 := by

  let AO  := 2 * r * Real.sqrt 2
  let AB  := 3 * r
  let AP  := 2 * (2 * r * Real.sqrt 2)
  let AD  := 2 * r * Real.sqrt 2
  let PB  := 3 * r - 2 * (2 * r * Real.sqrt 2)
  let DE  := 2 * r * Real.sqrt 2
  let OB  := r
  ¬(AP ^ 2 = PB * AB) ∧ 
  ¬(AP * r = PB * AD) ∧ 
  ¬(AB ^ 2 = AD * DE) ∧ 
  ¬(AB * AD = OB * AO) := by

theorem sales_gain_percentage 
  (sp1 sp2 sp3  : ℝ) (profit1 profit2 profit3  : ℝ)
  (h_sp1  : sp1 = 195)
  (h_sp2  : sp2 = 330)
  (h_sp3  : sp3 = 120)
  (h_profit1  : profit1 = 45)
  (h_profit2  : profit2 = 80)
  (h_profit3  : profit3 = 30)  :
  let cp1  := sp1 - profit1
  let cp2  := sp2 - profit2
  let cp3  := sp3 - profit3
  let total_cp  := cp1 + cp2 + cp3
  let total_sp  := sp1 + sp2 + sp3
  let total_profit  := total_sp - total_cp
  let gain_percentage  := (total_profit / total_cp) * 100
  abs (gain_percentage - 31.63) < 0.01 := by

  let c_inv  := (9/2) * x -- C's investment

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 0
open BigOperators Real Nat Topology Rat
e3128700.theorem:
theorem tetrahedron_volume_height_2  : 
  let A₁  : ℝ × ℝ × ℝ  := (2, -1, -2)
  let A₂  : ℝ × ℝ × ℝ  := (1, 2, 1)
  let A₃  : ℝ × ℝ × ℝ  := (5, 0, -6)
  let A₄  : ℝ × ℝ × ℝ  := (-10, 9, -7)
  let v12  : ℝ × ℝ × ℝ  := (A₂.1 - A₁.1, A₂.2.1 - A₁.2.1, A₂.2.2 - A₁.2.2)  -- A₁A₂
  let v13  : ℝ × ℝ × ℝ  := (A₃.1 - A₁.1, A₃.2.1 - A₁.2.1, A₃.2.2 - A₁.2.2)  -- A₁A₃
  let v14  : ℝ × ℝ × ℝ  := (A₄.1 - A₁.1, A₄.2.1 - A₁.2.1, A₄.2.2 - A₁.2.2)  -- A₁A₄
  -- Cross product of v12 and v13
  let cross12_13  : ℝ × ℝ × ℝ  := (
    v12.2.1 * v13.2.2 - v12.2.2 * v13.2.1,
    v12.2.2 * v13.1 - v12.1 * v13.2.2,
    v12.1 * v13.2.1 - v12.2.1 * v13.1)
  -- Volume formula
  let volume  := |(v14.1 * cross12_13.1 + v14.2.1 * cross12_13.2.1 + 
    v14.2.2 * cross12_13.2.2)| / 6
  -- Height formula
  let height  := 3 * volume / (Real.sqrt (cross12_13.1^2 + cross12_13.2.1^2 + 
    cross12_13.2.2^2) / 2)
  -- Conclusions
  volume = 140/3 ∧ height = 4 * Real.sqrt 14 := by

    let big_total_wire_length  := pieces_per_cube * big_wire_length
    let big_volume  := big_wire_length ^ 3
    let small_volume  := small_wire_length ^ 3

theorem parallelogram_area_and_unit_vector  : 
  let u  : Fin 3 → ℝ  := ![2, 4, -3]
  let v  : Fin 3 → ℝ  := ![-1, 5, 2]
  let cross  := ![
    (u 1 * v 2 - u 2 * v 1),
    (u 2 * v 0 - u 0 * v 2),
    (u 0 * v 1 - u 1 * v 0)
  ]
  let magnitude  := Real.sqrt (23^2 + (-1)^2 + 14^2)
  let unit_vector  := fun i => (cross i) / magnitude
  (magnitude = Real.sqrt 726) ∧ 
  (∀ i, unit_vector i = match i with
    | 0 => 23/Real.sqrt 726
    | 1 => -1/Real.sqrt 726
    | 2 => 14/Real.sqrt 726) := by

theorem work_hours_solution
    (original_hours  : ℝ  := 25)
    (total_weeks  : ℝ  := 8)
    (target_amount  : ℝ  := 3000)
    (rate_increase  : ℝ  := 0.2)
    (missed_week  : ℝ  := 1)
    (h_pos  : original_hours > 0)
    (hw_pos  : total_weeks > 0)
    (ht_pos  : target_amount > 0)  :
    let new_hours  : ℝ  := 24
    let new_weeks  : ℝ  := total_weeks - missed_week
    let original_rate  : ℝ  := target_amount / (total_weeks * original_hours)
    let new_rate  : ℝ  := original_rate * (1 + rate_increase)
    new_hours * new_weeks * new_rate ≥ target_amount := by

  (price_tc_blender  : ℝ  := 2000) -- Price of blender at Technik-City
  (price_tc_grinder  : ℝ  := 4000) -- Price of meat grinder at Technik-City
  (price_tm_blender  : ℝ  := 1500) -- Price of blender at Technomarket
  (price_tm_grinder  : ℝ  := 4800) -- Price of meat grinder at Technomarket
  (discount_tc  : ℝ  := 0.1)       -- 10% discount at Technik-City
   : let total_tc  := price_tc_blender + price_tc_grinder
    let total_tm  := price_tm_blender + price_tm_grinder
    let final_price_tc  := total_tc * (1 - discount_tc)
    let final_price_tm  := total_tm
    final_price_tc < final_price_tm := by

  (n  : ℕ) 
  (h_n  : n > 1)
  (pieces  : Finset (ℕ × ℕ))
  (h_pieces  : pieces.card = n - 1)
  (h_valid_pos  : ∀ (p  : ℕ × ℕ), p ∈ pieces → p.1 < n ∧ p.2 < n)
  (h_no_adjacent  : ∀ (p q  : ℕ × ℕ), p ∈ pieces → q ∈ pieces → 
    p ≠ q → (p.1 + 1 ≠ q.1 ∨ p.2 ≠ q.2) ∧ 
    (p.1 ≠ q.1 ∨ p.2 + 1 ≠ q.2))  :
  ∃ (p  : ℕ × ℕ), p ∈ pieces ∧ 
  (∃ (q  : ℕ × ℕ), (q.1 = p.1 + 1 ∧ q.2 = p.2) ∨ 
                    (q.1 = p.1 ∧ q.2 = p.2 + 1) ∨
                    (q.1 = p.1 - 1 ∧ q.2 = p.2) ∨
                    (q.1 = p.1 ∧ q.2 = p.2 - 1)) ∧ 
  q.1 < n ∧ q.2 < n ∧ q ∉ pieces := by

  (x₁  : ℝ) (y₁  : ℝ) (h_on_c1  : x₁^2/4 + y₁^2 = 1)
  -- Let's define a point on C₂

   : 1 ≤ Real.sqrt ((x₁ - x₂)^2 + (y₁ - y₂)^2) ∧

import Mathlib

open BigOperators Real Nat Topology Rat
cb116d16.theorem:
theorem linda_travel_distance  : 
  -- Initial time per mile is 12 minutes
  let x  : ℕ  := 12
  -- Time per mile increases by 4 minutes each day
  let times  : Fin 5 → ℕ  := fun i => x + 4 * i
  -- Distance covered each day (in miles) is 60 divided by minutes per mile
  let distances  : Fin 5 → ℕ  := fun i => 60 / (times i)
  -- The sum of all distances equals 15
  ∑ i  : Fin 5, distances i = 15 := by

  (h_triangle  : a < b + c ∧ b < a + c ∧ c < a + b) -- triangle inequality
  (h_cos  : cos A = (b^2 + c^2 - a^2)/(2*b*c)) -- law of cosines

b31ca241.theorem:
theorem tangent_line_at_one_157  : let f (x  : ℝ)  := -x^2 + 1
                             let f' (x  : ℝ)  := -2*x
                             let p  := (1  : ℝ)
                             let tangent_line (x  : ℝ)  := -2*x + 2
                             -- f' is the derivative of f
                             (∀ x, HasDerivAt f (f' x) x) →
                             -- The point (1,0) lies on the curve
                             f p = 0 →
                             -- The tangent line passes through (1,0)
                             tangent_line p = 0 →
                             -- The slope of the tangent line equals f'(1)
                             f' p = -2 →
                             -- Therefore the tangent line equation is y = -2x + 2
                             ∀ x, tangent_line x = -2*x + 2 := by

  (∀ a b  : ℝ, a < 0 → b < 0 → ¬(Real.sqrt a * Real.sqrt b = Real.sqrt (a * b))) ∧ 
  -- Statement B is incorrect (sqrt(6)/sqrt(3) = sqrt(2))
  (Real.sqrt 6 / Real.sqrt 3 = Real.sqrt 2) ∧
  -- Statement C is incorrect (3 + 2*sqrt(3) ≠ 5*sqrt(3))
  (3 + 2 * Real.sqrt 3 ≠ 5 * Real.sqrt 3) ∧
  -- Statement D is correct (4*sqrt(2) - sqrt(2) = 3*sqrt(2))
  (4 * Real.sqrt 2 - Real.sqrt 2 = 3 * Real.sqrt 2) := by

theorem income_tax_calculation_7  : 
  let base_income  := 3000
  let deductible  := 800
  let taxable_amount  := base_income - deductible
  let tax_amount  := if taxable_amount ≤ 500 then 
                      0.05 * taxable_amount
                    else if taxable_amount ≤ 2000 then
                      0.1 * (taxable_amount - 500) + 0.05 * 500
                    else
                      0.15 * (taxable_amount - 2000) + 0.1 * 1500 + 0.05 * 500
  tax_amount = 205 := by

  (α β  : G.Plane) (a b  : G.Line) (h_diff_planes  : α ≠ β) (h_diff_lines  : a ≠ b)  :
  -- Proposition 1 is true
  (G.subset_line_plane b α ∧ G.not_subset_line_plane a α) →
    (G.parallel_line_line a b → G.parallel_line_plane a α) ∧
    ¬(G.parallel_line_plane a α → G.parallel_line_line a b) ∧
  -- Proposition 2 is false
  ¬(G.subset_line_plane a α ∧ G.subset_line_plane b α →
    (G.parallel_plane_plane α β → (G.parallel_plane_plane α β ∧ G.parallel_line_line b b))) := by

  let vertical_lines  : ℕ  := w + 1   -- number of vertical lines
  let horizontal_toothpicks  : ℕ  := horizontal_lines * w  -- total horizontal toothpicks
  let vertical_toothpicks  : ℕ  := vertical_lines * h     -- total vertical toothpicks

theorem matrix_sequence_existence (A B  : Matrix (Fin 2) (Fin 2) ℤ)
  (pos_A  : ∀ i j, 0 < A i j) (pos_B  : ∀ i j, 0 < B i j)
  (row_sums  : ∀ i, ∑ j, A i j = ∑ j, B i j)
  (col_sums  : ∀ j, ∑ i, A i j = ∑ i, B i j)  :
  ∃ n  : ℕ, ∃ (sequence  : Fin (n + 1) → Matrix (Fin 2) (Fin 2) ℤ),
    sequence 0 = A ∧ sequence n = B ∧
    ∀ i  : Fin n, ∃ (m j k n  : Fin 2),
      let diff  := sequence (i + 1) - sequence i
      (∀ x y, diff x y = 0 ∨ 
        ((x = m ∧ y = j) ∨ (x = m ∧ y = k) ∨ (x = n ∧ y = j) ∨ (x = n ∧ y = k))) ∧
      (diff m j = 1 ∧ diff m k = -1 ∧ diff n j = -1 ∧ diff n k = 1 ∨
       diff m j = -1 ∧ diff m k = 1 ∧ diff n j = 1 ∧ diff n k = -1) := by

  -- The negation of P
  let NotP  := ∃ t1 t2, HasEqualArea t1 t2 ∧ ¬IsCongruent t1 t2
  -- The converse of P
  let ConverseP  := ∀ t1 t2, ¬HasEqualArea t1 t2 → ¬IsCongruent t1 t2

  True := by

  let woman_rate  : ℚ  := 1/18  -- woman's work rate per day
  let total_rate  : ℚ  := 1/3   -- combined rate of all three

    (hβ  : 0 < β) (hβ_upper  : β < 180)
    (hγ  : 0 < γ) (hγ_upper  : γ < 180)  :
    ∃ (a b c  : ℝ),
    -- Side lengths are positive
    a > 0 ∧ b > 0 ∧ c > 0 ∧

    (k  : ℝ) (k_pos  : k > 0)
    (on_ox  : M.1 ≥ O.1 ∧ M.2 = O.2) -- M is on positive OX
    (on_oy  : N.1 = O.1 ∧ N.2 ≥ O.2) -- N is on positive OY
    (on_ox_m'  : M'.1 ≥ O.1 ∧ M'.2 = O.2) -- M' is on positive OX
    (on_oy_n'  : N'.1 = O.1 ∧ N'.2 ≥ O.2) -- N' is on positive OY
    (sum_const  : dist O M + dist O N = k) -- |OM| + |ON| = k
    (m'_dist  : dist O M' = k/2) -- |OM'| = k/2
    (n'_dist  : dist O N' = k/2) -- |ON'| = k/2
    (is_midpoint  : P = ((M.1 + N.1)/2, (M.2 + N.2)/2)) -- P is midpoint of MN
     : ∃ (t  : ℝ), t ∈ (Set.Icc 0 1) ∧ 
      P = ((1-t) • M' + t • N') := by

  let p  : ℝ × ℝ → ℝ  := fun x => x.2^2 - 4*x.1  -- parabola equation y² = 4x
  let f  : ℝ × ℝ  := (1, 0)  -- focus point
  let k  : ℝ  := Real.sqrt 3 / 3  -- slope of line
  let line  : ℝ × ℝ → ℝ  := fun x => x.2 - k*(x.1 - f.1)  -- line equation
  let x₁  := (14 + Real.sqrt 192)/2  -- first intersection x-coordinate
  let x₂  := (14 - Real.sqrt 192)/2  -- second intersection x-coordinate
  let y₁  := k*(x₁ - 1)  -- first intersection y-coordinate
  let y₂  := k*(x₂ - 1)  -- second intersection y-coordinate
  let A  := (x₁, y₁)  -- first intersection point
  let B  := (x₂, y₂)  -- second intersection point
  Real.sqrt ((x₁ - x₂)^2 + (y₁ - y₂)^2) = 16 := by

d18e7866.theorem:
theorem vector_dot_product_problem_40
    (x  : ℝ)  :
    let m  : ℝ × ℝ  := (Real.sqrt 3 * Real.cos x, -1)
    let n  : ℝ × ℝ  := (Real.sin x, Real.cos x ^ 2)
    let dot_product  := m.1 * n.1 + m.2 * n.2
    (x = Real.pi / 3 → dot_product = 1/2) ∧
    (x ∈ Set.Icc 0 (Real.pi / 4) →
      dot_product = Real.sqrt 3 / 3 - 1/2 →
      Real.cos (2 * x) = (3 * Real.sqrt 2 - Real.sqrt 3) / 6) := by

theorem lean_workbook_plus_16543_4  (chickenCost porkCost beefCost lambCost  : ℝ)
  (chickenDisc porkDisc beefDisc lambDisc  : ℝ)
  (h₀  : chickenCost = 4 * 3.47)
  (h₁  : porkCost = 6 * 1.28)
  (h₂  : beefCost = 8 * 2.37)
  (h₃  : lambCost = 9 * 0.85)
  (h₄  : chickenDisc = 5 / 100 * chickenCost)
  (h₅  : porkDisc = 5 / 100 * porkCost)
  (h₆  : beefDisc = 5 / 100 * beefCost)
  (h₇  : lambDisc = 5 / 100 * lambCost)
  (h₈  : 3.47 > 2)  -- Chicken quantity is more than 2 kg, so the discount applies
  (h₉  : 2.37 > 2)  -- Beef quantity is more than 2 kg, so the discount applies
  (h₁₀  : 1.28 < 2) -- Pork quantity is less than 2 kg, so no discount
  (h₁₁  : 0.85 < 2) -- Lamb quantity is less than 2 kg, so no discount
  (h₁₂  : 0 < 5 / 100) -- The discount rate is 5%, which is greater than 0
  (h₁₃  : 0 < 4 * 3.47) -- The cost of chicken is greater than 0
  (h₁₄  : 0 < 6 * 1.28) -- The cost of pork is greater than 0
  (h₁₅  : 0 < 8 * 2.37) -- The cost of beef is greater than 0
  (h₁₆  : 0 < 9 * 0.85) -- The cost of lamb is greater than 0
   : (chickenCost - chickenDisc) + (porkCost - porkDisc) + (beefCost - beefDisc) + (lambCost - lambDisc) = 46.528 := by

theorem reflected_ray_slope  : 
  let A  : ℝ × ℝ  := (-2, -3)
  let A'  : ℝ × ℝ  := (2, -3)  -- symmetric point of A about y-axis
  let C  : ℝ × ℝ  := (-3, 2)   -- center of circle
  let r  : ℝ  := 1            -- radius of circle
  ∀ k  : ℝ, (k ≠ 0) →        -- slope of potential line
  -- If the line y + 3 = k(x - 2) is tangent to the circle
  (abs (-3*k - 2 - 2*k - 3) / Real.sqrt (k^2 + 1) = 1) →
  k = -4/3 ∨ k = -3/4 := by

theorem min_B_value (n  : ℕ) (hn  : n = 13)  :
  -- B = 4097 is the smallest natural number B such that
  -- there are exactly n positive integers greater than √15 and less than ∛B
  (∀ B < 4097, ¬(∃ S  : Finset ℕ,
    (S.card = n) ∧
    (∀ k ∈ S, k > Real.sqrt 15 ∧ k < B ^ (1/3)) ∧
    (∀ k  : ℕ, k > Real.sqrt 15 ∧ k < B ^ (1/3) → k ∈ S)))
  ∧
  (∃ S  : Finset ℕ,
    (S.card = n) ∧
    (∀ k ∈ S, k > Real.sqrt 15 ∧ k < 4097 ^ (1/3)) ∧
    (∀ k  : ℕ, k > Real.sqrt 15 ∧ k < 4097 ^ (1/3) → k ∈ S)) := by

25024d30.theorem:
theorem circle_central_symmetry (O Q  : ℝ × ℝ) (R  : ℝ) (X  : ℝ × ℝ)  :
  let X₁  := (2 * O.1 - X.1, 2 * O.2 - X.2) -- X₁ is reflection of X through O
  let Q₁  := (2 * O.1 - Q.1, 2 * O.2 - Q.2) -- Q₁ is reflection of Q through O
  dist X Q = R → -- X is on circle S
  dist X₁ Q₁ = R -- X₁ is on circle S₁ := by

    (h_b  : b = 1)

theorem line_intersection_value_2 (a  : ℝ)  : 
  -- Define the slopes of the three lines
  let slope1  := 1 -- slope of x-y+1=0
  let slope2  := -2 -- slope of 2x+y-4=0
  let slope3  := a -- slope of ax-y+2=0
  -- The condition that exactly two points of intersection exist
  (∃! n  : ℕ, n = 2 ∧ 
    -- Two lines are parallel (have equal slopes)
    ((slope1 = slope3 ∨ slope2 = slope3) ∧
    -- And they have different y-intercepts
    (slope1 ≠ slope2 ∧ slope1 ≠ slope3 ∧ slope2 ≠ slope3))) →
  a = 1 ∨ a = -2 := by

theorem island_problem_3 (n  : ℕ) 
    (h_n  : n ≤ 10) 
    (statements  : Fin 10 → Bool)  -- True for Statement 1, False for Statement 2
    (h_count  : (Finset.univ.filter (fun i => statements i)).card = 5) 
    (h_count_false  : (Finset.univ.filter (fun i => ¬statements i)).card = 5)
    (is_knight  : Fin 10 → Bool)  -- True for knight, False for liar
    (h_statement1_true  : ∀ i, statements i = true → 
      ¬∃ j, j.val > i.val ∧ is_knight j = true)
    (h_statement2_true  : ∀ i, statements i = false → 
      ¬∃ j, j.val < i.val ∧ is_knight j = false)
    (h_statement1_false  : ∀ i, statements i = false → 
      ∃ j, j.val > i.val ∧ is_knight j = true)
    (h_statement2_false  : ∀ i, statements i = true → 
      ∃ j, j.val < i.val ∧ is_knight j = false)  : 
    (Finset.univ.filter (fun i => is_knight i)).card ≤ 6 := by

    (third_rate  : ℕ  := 5)
    (fourth_rate  : ℕ  := 6)

    (court_costs  : ℕ  := 350)

      ((base_fine +
       (10 * first_rate) +

      court_costs) / 10 +
      (lawyer_rate * lawyer_hours) = 1346 := by

7e564184.theorem:
theorem geometric_sequence_condition  :
    ∃ (a₁ q  : ℝ), -- Find counterexample for necessity
      (∀ n  : ℕ, n > 0 → a₁ * q^n > a₁ * q^(n-1)) ∧  -- conclusion holds
      ¬(a₁ > 0 ∧ q > 1) ∧                           -- condition fails
    ∀ (a₁ q  : ℝ),                                   -- sufficiency part
      (a₁ > 0 ∧ q > 1) →                           -- if condition holds
      ∀ n  : ℕ, n > 0 → a₁ * q^n > a₁ * q^(n-1)     -- then conclusion holds := by

  -- B's claim : "At least one of us is a knight and this island is not Maya"
  (w.b_is_knight = ((w.a_is_knight ∨ w.b_is_knight) ∧ ¬w.is_maya)) →

247bd50e.theorem:
theorem clock_angle_seven_twentyfive_2  :
  let hours  : ℝ  := 7
  let minutes  : ℝ  := 25
  let degrees_per_hour  : ℝ  := 360 / 12
  let degrees_per_minute  : ℝ  := 360 / 60
  let hour_hand_angle  : ℝ  := hours * degrees_per_hour + (minutes / 60) * degrees_per_hour
  let minute_hand_angle  : ℝ  := minutes * degrees_per_minute
  let angle_between  : ℝ  := |hour_hand_angle - minute_hand_angle|
  let obtuse_angle  : ℝ  := max angle_between (360 - angle_between)
  obtuse_angle = 287.5 := by

    (hs  : 0 < s)
    (A  : Fin s → Finset (Fin n))
    (B  : Fin t → Finset (Fin n))
    (h_equal_pairs  : ∀ x y  : Fin n, x < y →
      (Finset.filter (fun i => {x, y} ⊆ A i) (Finset.univ  : Finset (Fin s))).card =
      (Finset.filter (fun i => {x, y} ⊆ B i) (Finset.univ  : Finset (Fin t))).card)  :
    ∃ x  : Fin n,
      (Finset.filter (fun i => x ∈ A i) (Finset.univ  : Finset (Fin s))).card ≥

    -- The marked cells are within the board
    (∀ (i j  : ℕ), (i, j) ∈ marked_cells → i < 8 ∧ j < 9) ∧
    -- There are exactly 14 marked cells
    marked_cells.card = 14 ∧
    -- Every 5 consecutive cells (horizontally, vertically, or diagonally) contains a marked cell
    (∀ (i j  : ℕ) (dir  : Bool) (start  : ℕ),
      i < 8 ∧ j < 9 ∧ start < 5 →

theorem least_multiple_prime_factors  : 
  let p₄  := 7  -- fourth smallest prime
  let p₅  := 11 -- fifth smallest prime
  let p₆  := 13 -- sixth smallest prime
  let product  := p₄ * p₅ * p₆
  -- The following conditions are true
  ∀ n  : ℕ, 
    n > 0 ∧                           -- n is positive
    n ≤ p₆.factorial ∧                -- n is at most the factorial of largest prime
    p₄ ∣ n ∧ p₅ ∣ n ∧ p₆ ∣ n         -- n is divisible by all required primes
    → n ≥ product := by

  (M N  : ℝ × ℝ)       -- Intersection points
  (hM  : C M ∧ l M)    -- M lies on both circle and line
  (hN  : C N ∧ l N)    -- N lies on both circle and line
  (hMN  : Real.sqrt ((M.1 - N.1)^2 + (M.2 - N.2)^2) = 4/Real.sqrt 5)  -- Distance MN = 4/√5

   : m = 4 := by

  let odd_days  := 10 -- number of odd days
  let even_days  := 10 -- number of even days
  let pumpkin_cans_per_pie  := 2 -- cans needed per pumpkin pie
  let apple_cans_per_pie  := 1 -- cans needed per apple pie
  let chocolate_cans_per_cake  := 3 -- cans needed per chocolate cake
  let lemon_cans_per_tart  := (3/2  : ℚ) -- cans needed per lemon tart
  let eaten_pumpkin  := 2 -- pumpkin pies eaten
  let eaten_apple  := 5 -- apple pies eaten
  let eaten_chocolate  := 1 -- chocolate cakes eaten
  let eaten_lemon  := 1 -- lemon tarts eaten
  ↑252 = ⌈(
    -- Remaining pumpkin pies
    (odd_days * 3 + even_days * 2 - eaten_pumpkin) * pumpkin_cans_per_pie +
    -- Remaining apple pies
    (odd_days * 2 + even_days * 4 - eaten_apple) * apple_cans_per_pie +
    -- Remaining chocolate cakes
    (odd_days * 1 + even_days * 2 - eaten_chocolate) * chocolate_cans_per_cake +
    -- Remaining lemon tarts
    (even_days * 1 - eaten_lemon) * lemon_cans_per_tart
  )⌉ := by

theorem triangle_area_counterintuitive  : ∃ (a₁ b₁ c₁ a₂ b₂ c₂ h₁ h₂  : ℝ),
  -- First triangle sides
  a₁ > 0 ∧ b₁ > 0 ∧ c₁ > 0 ∧
  -- Second triangle sides
  a₂ > 0 ∧ b₂ > 0 ∧ c₂ > 0 ∧
  -- All sides of first triangle shorter than second triangle
  a₁ < a₂ ∧ b₁ < b₂ ∧ c₁ < c₂ ∧
  -- Both triangles are valid (triangle inequality)
  a₁ + b₁ > c₁ ∧ b₁ + c₁ > a₁ ∧ c₁ + a₁ > b₁ ∧
  a₂ + b₂ > c₂ ∧ b₂ + c₂ > a₂ ∧ c₂ + a₂ > b₂ ∧
  -- First triangle area is greater
  (1/2 * a₁ * h₁) > (1/2 * a₂ * h₂) := by

  let almond_crack_percent  : ℚ  := 80/100

  let cashew_salt_percent  : ℚ  := 70/100
  let cashew_open_percent  : ℚ  := 60/100
  True → 
  -- Total nuts with shells and opened/cracked shells = 120
  (total_pistachios * (pistachio_shell_percent  : ℚ) * (pistachio_open_percent  : ℚ)).floor +
  (total_almonds * (almond_shell_percent  : ℚ) * (almond_crack_percent  : ℚ)).floor +
  (total_cashews * (cashew_shell_percent  : ℚ) * (cashew_open_percent  : ℚ)).floor = 120 ∧
  -- Salted cashews count = 23
  (total_cashews * (cashew_shell_percent  : ℚ) * (cashew_salt_percent  : ℚ)).floor = 23 := by

      (∀ i  : ℕ, 1 ≤ i ∧ i ≤ n ↔ partition.join.count i = 1) ∧

6edd8b24.theorem:
theorem sin3x_odd_and_periodic  : 
  -- State that sin(3x) is odd
  (∀ x  : ℝ, sin (3 * (-x)) = -(sin (3 * x))) ∧ 
  -- State that sin(3x) is periodic with period 2π
  (∀ x  : ℝ, sin (3 * (x + 2 * Real.pi)) = sin (3 * x)) ∧ 
  -- State that x³ is not periodic
  (∃ ε  : ℝ, ε > 0 ∧ ∃ x  : ℝ, (x + ε)^3 ≠ x^3 + ε) ∧
  -- State that cos(2x) is not odd
  (∃ x  : ℝ, cos (2 * (-x)) ≠ -(cos (2 * x))) ∧
  -- State that tan(2x + π/4) is not odd
  (∃ x  : ℝ, tan (2 * (-x) + Real.pi/4) ≠ -(tan (2 * x + Real.pi/4))) := by

    (weeks_per_month  : ℕ  := 4)   -- weeks in a month
    (num_children  : ℕ  := 4)      -- number of children
    (child_share  : ℕ  := 1680)    -- amount each child receives
     : let total_weekly  := husband_weekly + wife_weekly
      let monthly_savings  := total_weekly * weeks_per_month
      let total_savings  := monthly_savings * 6
      2 * (num_children * child_share) = total_savings := by

theorem isosceles_45_deg_not_always_similar  : 
  ∃ (t1 t2  : ℝ × ℝ × ℝ), 
    -- t1 and t2 are valid triangle angle triples
    (t1.1 + t1.2.1 + t1.2.2 = 180) ∧ 
    (t2.1 + t2.2.1 + t2.2.2 = 180) ∧
    -- Both triangles are isosceles
    (t1.1 = t1.2.1) ∧
    (t2.1 = t2.2.1) ∧
    -- Both triangles have a 45° angle
    (t1.1 = 45 ∨ t1.2.2 = 45) ∧
    (t2.1 = 45 ∨ t2.2.2 = 45) ∧
    -- But they are not similar
    ¬(t1.1 = t2.1 ∧ t1.2.1 = t2.2.1 ∧ t1.2.2 = t2.2.2) := by

3a0b80c0.theorem:
theorem curve_intersection_solution  :
    let C₁_equation (x y  : ℝ) (a  : ℝ)  := x - y = a - 1
    let C₂_equation (x y  : ℝ)  := y^2 = 4*x
    let P (a  : ℝ)  := (a, 1)
    let param_to_point (a t  : ℝ)  := (a + Real.sqrt 2 * t, 1 + Real.sqrt 2 * t)
    ∃ t₁ t₂  : ℝ, 
      let A  := param_to_point (1/36) t₁
      let B  := param_to_point (1/36) t₂
      t₁ = 2*t₂ ∧                              -- t₁ = 2t₂
      t₁ + t₂ = Real.sqrt 2 ∧                  -- sum of parameters
      C₁_equation A.1 A.2 (1/36) ∧             -- A lies on C₁
      C₁_equation B.1 B.2 (1/36) ∧             -- B lies on C₂
      C₂_equation A.1 A.2 ∧                    -- A lies on C₂
      C₂_equation B.1 B.2 := by

d1764ef4.theorem:
theorem cosine_sine_translation_symmetry  :
  -- For the function f(x) = cos(2x) - sin(2x)
  -- If we translate it left by m units and get symmetry about origin
  -- Then m = π/8 is the minimum positive value that achieves this
  let f (x  : ℝ)  := Real.cos (2*x) - Real.sin (2*x)
  let g (m x  : ℝ)  := f (x + m)  -- translated function
  let h (m x  : ℝ)  := g m (-x)   -- reflection about origin
  ∀ m  : ℝ, m > 0 →
  (∀ x  : ℝ, g m x = h m x) →    -- symmetry condition
  m ≥ Real.pi/8 := by

cd6bd371.header:
import Mathlib
import Aesop
set_option maxHeartbeats 0
open BigOperators Real Nat Topology Rat
cd6bd371.theorem:
theorem card_draw_probability_same_rank  : 
  let total_cards  : ℕ  := 52
  let total_ranks  : ℕ  := 13
  let cards_per_rank  : ℕ  := 4
  let drawn_cards  : ℕ  := 5
  -- Total number of ways to draw 5 cards
  let total_ways  := choose total_cards drawn_cards
  -- Number of ways to draw all different ranks
  let diff_ranks_ways  := choose total_ranks drawn_cards * (cards_per_rank ^ drawn_cards)
  -- Probability of drawing all different ranks
  let diff_ranks_prob  : ℚ  := diff_ranks_ways / total_ways
  -- Probability of drawing at least two cards with same rank
  let same_rank_prob  : ℚ  := 1 - diff_ranks_prob
  |same_rank_prob - 49/100| < 1/100 := by

5b4864b3.theorem:
theorem rotation_exists_for_skew_lines 
    (A A'  : ℝ × ℝ × ℝ) -- Points A and A' in 3D space
    (a a'  : Set (ℝ × ℝ × ℝ)) -- Lines a and a'
    (h_A  : A ∈ a) -- A lies on line a
    (h_A'  : A' ∈ a') -- A' lies on line a'
    (h_skew  : ∃ p ∈ a, ∀ q ∈ a', ¬(p = q)) -- Lines are skew
     : ∃ (l  : Set (ℝ × ℝ × ℝ)), -- There exists a rotation axis l
    (∀ x ∈ l, ∀ y ∈ l, x = y) ∧ -- l is a line
    (∃ α β  : Set (ℝ × ℝ × ℝ), -- There exist reflection planes α and β
      l = α ∩ β ∧ -- l is their intersection
      A' ∈ (α ∪ {x | ∃ y ∈ α, y = x}) ∧ -- A' can be obtained by reflection over α
      A' ∈ (β ∪ {x | ∃ y ∈ β, y = x})) -- A' can be obtained by reflection over β := by

  (years  : ℕ  := 60)
  (months  : ℕ  := 50)
  (weeks  : ℕ  := 40)
  (days  : ℕ  := 30)
  (months_in_year  : ℕ  := 12)
  (min_days_in_month  : ℕ  := 29)
  (max_days_in_month  : ℕ  := 31)
  (days_in_week  : ℕ  := 7)
  (days_in_year  : ℕ  := 365)  :
  let extra_years  := months / months_in_year
  let extra_months  := months % months_in_year
  let min_total_days  := extra_months * min_days_in_month + weeks * days_in_week + days
  let max_total_days  := extra_months * max_days_in_month + weeks * days_in_week + days
  years + extra_years + 1 = 65 := by

    (hO  : O = (0, 0)) -- Origin position
    (hAF  : ‖A - F‖ = 8 * ‖O - F‖) -- Given condition
    (hpara  : ∀ P  : ℝ × ℝ, P ∈ {P | (P.2)^2 = 2 * p * P.1} → 
      ‖P - F‖ = ‖P - ((-p), 0)‖) -- Parabola definition

  let op  : ℝ × ℝ  := (2, 1)
  let oa  : ℝ × ℝ  := (1, 7)
  let ob  : ℝ × ℝ  := (5, 1)
  -- For any point X on line OP
  ∀ X  : ℝ × ℝ, (∃ t  : ℝ, X = (2*t, t)) →
  -- The dot product of XA and XB is greater than or equal to -8
  ((X.1 - oa.1) * (X.1 - ob.1) + (X.2 - oa.2) * (X.2 - ob.2)) ≥ -8 ∧
  -- And there exists a point X where this minimum is achieved
  ∃ X  : ℝ × ℝ, (∃ t  : ℝ, X = (2*t, t)) ∧
    ((X.1 - oa.1) * (X.1 - ob.1) + (X.2 - oa.2) * (X.2 - ob.2)) = -8 := by

  let f (x  : ℝ)  := x^2 + 1  -- First curve
  let g (x  : ℝ)  := Real.sqrt (x - 1)  -- Second curve
  let dist (p q  : ℝ × ℝ)  := Real.sqrt ((p.1 - q.1)^2 + (p.2 - q.2)^2)  -- Distance function
  ∃ d  : ℝ, d = (3 * Real.sqrt 2) / 4 ∧ 
    (∀ x y  : ℝ, x ≥ 0 → y ≥ 1 → 
      dist (x, f x) (y, g y) ≥ d) ∧
    (∃ x₀ y₀  : ℝ, x₀ ≥ 0 ∧ y₀ ≥ 1 ∧ 
      dist (x₀, f x₀) (y₀, g y₀) = d) := by

  (perp_BD_BC  : (D.1 - B.1) * (C.1 - B.1) + (D.2 - B.2) * (C.2 - B.2) = 0) -- BD ⊥ BC

  (len_BC  : Real.sqrt ((C.1 - B.1)^2 + (C.2 - B.2)^2) = 20) -- BC = 20
  (len_CD  : Real.sqrt ((D.1 - C.1)^2 + (D.2 - C.2)^2) = 9) -- CD = 9

    (h_f₁  : ∀ x, f₁ x = x^3 - (α + β + γ)*x^2 + (α*β + α*γ + β*γ)*x - α*β*γ)
    (a₁ b₁ c₁  : ℝ)
    (h_a₁  : a₁ = -(α + β + γ))
    (h_b₁  : b₁ = α*β + α*γ + β*γ)
    (h_c₁  : c₁ = -α*β*γ)
    (a₂ b₂ c₂  : ℝ)
    (h_f₂  : ∀ x, (x*(x + b₁)^2 - (a₁*x + c₁)^2) = x^3 + a₂*x^2 + b₂*x + c₂)
    (h_a₂  : a₂ = -(α^2 + β^2 + γ^2))  : 
    let aₙ  := λ n => -(α^(2^(n-1)) + β^(2^(n-1)) + γ^(2^(n-1)))
    ∀ ε > 0, ∃ N, ∀ n ≥ N, |((-(aₙ n))^(1/2^(n-1))) - α| < ε := by

f3ad9385.theorem:
theorem circle_tangent_to_line_8  : 
  let line  := fun (x y  : ℝ) => x - y = 0
  let center  : ℝ × ℝ  := (-1, 3)
  let r  := 2 * Real.sqrt 2
  let circle  := fun (x y  : ℝ) => (x + 1)^2 + (y - 3)^2 = 8
  -- The circle equation is correct
  ∀ x y  : ℝ, circle x y ↔ 
    -- If and only if :
    -- 1) It's a circle with given center and radius
    ((x - center.1)^2 + (y - center.2)^2 = r^2) ∧
    -- 2) The distance from center to line equals radius
    (abs (center.1 - center.2) / Real.sqrt 2 = r) := by

  -- Exchange rates
  (euro_to_usd  : ℝ  := 1.20)
  (pound_to_usd  : ℝ  := 1.38)
  -- Family money in various currencies
  (aunt_usd  : ℝ  := 9)
  (uncle_euro  : ℝ  := 9)
  (sister_usd  : ℝ  := 7)
  -- Friends money in various currencies
  (friend1_usd  : ℝ  := 22)
  (friend2_usd  : ℝ  := 23)
  (friend3_euro  : ℝ  := 18)
  (friend4_pound  : ℝ  := 15)
  (friend5_usd  : ℝ  := 22)
  -- Weights
  (family_weight  : ℝ  := 0.40)
  (friends_weight  : ℝ  := 0.60)
   : let family_total  := aunt_usd + uncle_euro * euro_to_usd + sister_usd
    let friends_total  := friend1_usd + friend2_usd + friend3_euro * euro_to_usd +
                        friend4_pound * pound_to_usd + friend5_usd
    let weighted_mean  := family_total * family_weight + friends_total * friends_weight
    weighted_mean = 76.30 := by

05502654.theorem:
theorem cos_intersection_ratio_2  : 
  let cos60  : ℝ  := 1/2
  let intersection_points  : List ℝ  := [60, 300]
  let segment_lengths  : List ℝ  := [240, 120]
  -- All points are in [0, 360°]
  ∀ x ∈ intersection_points, 0 ≤ x ∧ x ≤ 360 →
  -- The cosine of these points equals 1/2
  ∀ x ∈ intersection_points, cos (x * Real.pi / 180) = cos60 →
  -- The segment lengths are correct
  segment_lengths[0]! = intersection_points[1]! - intersection_points[0]! →
  segment_lengths[1]! = 360 - intersection_points[1]! + intersection_points[0]! →
  -- The ratio of the segments is 2 :1
  segment_lengths[0]! / segment_lengths[1]! = 2 := by

theorem ice_cream_spending
  (base_price  : ℚ  := 4) -- Base price per pint is $4
  (sunday_pints  : ℕ  := 4) -- Pints bought on Sunday
  (monday_discount  : ℚ  := (1/10)) -- 10% discount on Monday
  (monday_multiplier  : ℕ  := 3) -- Monday purchases triple of Sunday
  (tuesday_discount  : ℚ  := (1/5)) -- 20% discount on Tuesday
  (tuesday_fraction  : ℚ  := (1/3)) -- Tuesday purchases one-third of Monday
  (return_fraction  : ℚ  := (1/2)) -- Half of Tuesday's purchase returned
   : (base_price * sunday_pints +
     base_price * (1 - monday_discount) * (sunday_pints * monday_multiplier) +
     base_price * (1 - tuesday_discount) * (sunday_pints * monday_multiplier * tuesday_fraction) -
     base_price * (1 - tuesday_discount) * (sunday_pints * monday_multiplier * tuesday_fraction * return_fraction)) = (6560/100) := by

    (area_eq  : (A.x * (B.y - C.y) + B.x * (C.y - A.y) + C.x * (A.y - B.y)) = 9) 
    (is_clockwise  : A.x * (B.y - C.y) + B.x * (C.y - A.y) + C.x * (A.y - B.y) > 0)
    (interior_points  : Set LatticePoint)

    (all_interior  : ∀ P ∈ interior_points, 
      ∃ (s  : ℚ), s > 0 ∧ s < 1 ∧
      P.x = ⌊s * A.x + (1 - s) * B.x⌋ ∧
      P.y = ⌊s * A.y + (1 - s) * B.y⌋)  :

    ∃ (t  : ℚ), t > 0 ∧ t < 1 ∧
    P.x = m + t * n := by

  -- Initial conditions
  (total_volume  : ℝ) (first_volume  : ℝ)
  (second_concentration  : ℝ) (final_concentration  : ℝ)
  (h_total  : total_volume = 200)
  (h_first  : first_volume = 75)
  (h_second  : second_concentration = 0.12)
  (h_final  : final_concentration = 0.15)
  (h_pos_total  : total_volume > 0)
  (h_pos_first  : first_volume > 0)
  (h_first_less  : first_volume < total_volume)  :
  -- Conclusion : The first solution's concentration must be 0.20 (20%)
  ∃ first_concentration  : ℝ,
    first_concentration = 0.20 ∧
    first_concentration * first_volume +
    second_concentration * (total_volume - first_volume) =
    final_concentration * total_volume := by

e5bc4aba.theorem:
theorem quadrilateral_reflection_property 
  (A B C D  : ℝ × ℝ) -- Four points forming the quadrilateral
  (is_convex  : (B.1 - A.1) * (C.2 - A.2) ≠ (C.1 - A.1) * (B.2 - A.2)) -- Convexity condition
   : ∃ (P  : ℝ × ℝ), -- There exists a point P such that
    (P = A ∨ P = B ∨ P = C ∨ P = D) ∧  -- P is one of the vertices
    ∃ (M  : ℝ × ℝ), -- There exists a midpoint M such that
    ((M = ((A.1 + B.1)/2, (A.2 + B.2)/2)) ∨  -- M is midpoint of AB
     (M = ((B.1 + C.1)/2, (B.2 + C.2)/2)) ∨  -- or M is midpoint of BC
     (M = ((C.1 + D.1)/2, (C.2 + D.2)/2)) ∨  -- or M is midpoint of CD
     (M = ((D.1 + A.1)/2, (D.2 + A.2)/2))) ∧  -- or M is midpoint of DA
    ∃ (R  : ℝ × ℝ), -- There exists a reflection point R such that
    (R = (2*M.1 - P.1, 2*M.2 - P.2)) ∧  -- R is reflection of P over M
    (∃ (inside  : Bool), inside = true) -- R is inside the quadrilateral := by

    (h_len  : scores1.length = 4) -- Verify initial length
    (h_valid1  : ∀ x ∈ scores1, x < 95) -- All scores below 95
    (h_unique1  : scores1.Nodup) -- Initial scores are unique
     : ∃ (a b  : Int),
      a = 93 ∧ b = 78 ∧  -- The two new scores
      (scores1 ++ [a, b]).Nodup ∧  -- All six scores are unique

  (a b  : ℕ → ℕ)
  (h₀  : 0 < n)
  (h₁  : ∀ i, a i ≠ b i)
  (h₂  : ∀ i, a i < b i)
  (h₃  : ∀ i, b i ≤ 2 * n)
  (h₄  : ∀ i, a i + 1 ≤ 2 * n)
  (h₅  : ∀ i, a i ≠ a (i + 1))
  (h₆  : ∀ i, b i ≠ b (i + 1))
  (h₇  : ∀ i, a i + 1 ≠ b (i + 1))
  (h₈  : ∀ i, b i + 1 ≠ a (i + 1))
  (h₉  : ∀ i, a i + 1 ≠ a i)
  (h₁₀  : ∀ i, b i + 1 ≠ b i)
  (h₁₁  : ∀ i, a i + 1 ≠ b i)
  (h₁₂  : ∀ i, b i + 1 ≠ a i)
  (h₁₃  : ∀ i, a i ≠ b i)
  (h₁₄  : ∀ i, a i < b i)
  (h₁₅  : ∀ i, b i ≤ 2 * n)
  (h₁₆  : ∀ i, a i + 1 ≤ 2 * n)
  (h₁₇  : ∀ i, a i ≠ a (i + 1))
  (h₁₈  : ∀ i, b i ≠ b (i + 1))
  (h₁₉  : ∀ i, a i + 1 ≠ b (i + 1))
  (h₂₀  : ∀ i, b i + 1 ≠ a (i + 1))
  (h₂₁  : ∀ i, a i + 1 ≠ a i)
  (h₂₂  : ∀ i, b i + 1 ≠ b i)
  (h₂₃  : ∀ i, a i + 1 ≠ b i)
  (h₂₄  : ∀ i, b i + 1 ≠ a i)
   : ∃ k, 0 < k ∧ 2 * n ≤ k ∧ ∃ v  : ℕ → ℕ, v 0 = a 0 ∧ v 1 = b 0 ∧ ∀ i, v (i + 2) = v i + 1 ∧ v (i + 1) ≠ v i + 1 := by

  (A B C D P Q K  : ℝ × ℝ) -- Points in the plane
  (angle_acute₁  : (B - A) • (C - A) > 0) -- Angle ABC is acute
  (angle_acute₂  : (C - A) • (B - A) > 0) -- Angle ACB is acute
  (D_on_BC  : ∃ t  : ℝ, 0 < t ∧ t < 1 ∧ D = B + t • (C - B)) -- D lies on BC
  (AD_bisector  : (D - A) • (P - B) = 0 ∧ (D - A) • (Q - C) = 0) -- AD bisects BAC
  (DP_perp  : (D - P) • (A - B) = 0) -- DP perpendicular to AB
  (DQ_perp  : (D - Q) • (A - C) = 0) -- DQ perpendicular to AC
  (K_intersect  : ∃ s t  : ℝ, 0 < s ∧ s < 1 ∧ 0 < t ∧ t < 1 ∧ 
    K = C + s • (P - C) ∧ K = B + t • (Q - B)) -- K is intersection of CP and BQ
   : (K - A) • (C - B) = 0 := by

theorem isosceles_triangle_side_length_14  : 
  ∀ (P Q R  : ℝ × ℝ) (S  : ℝ × ℝ), -- Points in R²
  -- Assumptions about the triangle
  let base  := dist P Q
  let height  := dist R S
  let side  := dist P R
  -- Main conditions
  base = 30 ∧                        -- Base length is 30
  height = 8 ∧                       -- Height is 8 (we include this as assumption to make conclusion shorter)
  (P.1 - Q.1) * (R.1 - S.1) + (P.2 - Q.2) * (R.2 - S.2) = 0 ∧  -- Right angle at S
  S.1 = (P.1 + Q.1) / 2 ∧          -- S is midpoint of PQ
  S.2 = (P.2 + Q.2) / 2 ∧          -- S is midpoint of PQ
  2 * (base * height) / 2 = 120     -- Area is 120
  →
  side = 17 := by

theorem ellipse_focal_distance_product_bounds (P  : ℝ × ℝ) 
    (h_on_ellipse  : P.1^2/4 + P.2^2 = 1)  : 
    let F₁  : ℝ × ℝ  := (-Real.sqrt 3, 0)
    let F₂  : ℝ × ℝ  := (Real.sqrt 3, 0)
    let d  := Real.sqrt (P.1^2 + P.2^2)  -- distance from center to P
    let prod  := Real.sqrt ((P.1 - F₁.1)^2 + (P.2 - F₁.2)^2) * 
                Real.sqrt ((P.1 - F₂.1)^2 + (P.2 - F₂.2)^2)
    1 ≤ prod ∧ prod ≤ 4 ∧ 
    (∃ P₁  : ℝ × ℝ, P₁.1^2/4 + P₁.2^2 = 1 ∧ 
      Real.sqrt ((P₁.1 - F₁.1)^2 + (P₁.2 - F₁.2)^2) * 
      Real.sqrt ((P₁.1 - F₂.1)^2 + (P₁.2 - F₂.2)^2) = 4) ∧
    (∃ P₂  : ℝ × ℝ, P₂.1^2/4 + P₂.2^2 = 1 ∧ 
      Real.sqrt ((P₂.1 - F₁.1)^2 + (P₂.2 - F₁.2)^2) * 
      Real.sqrt ((P₂.1 - F₂.1)^2 + (P₂.2 - F₂.2)^2) = 1) := by

    (h_no_triple  : ∀ (p1 p2 p3  : Fin 2 → ℕ), p1 ∈ segments → p2 ∈ segments → p3 ∈ segments → 
      p1 ≠ p2 → p2 ≠ p3 → p1 ≠ p3 → 
      ∃ (i j k  : Fin 2), p1 i ≠ p2 j ∧ p2 j ≠ p3 k ∧ p1 k ≠ p3 i)
    (h_single_intersect  : ∀ (p1 p2  : Fin 2 → ℕ), p1 ∈ segments → p2 ∈ segments → p1 ≠ p2 →

    ∃ (present_assignment  : Fin (2 * n - 1) → Fin (2 * n - 1)), 
    (Function.Bijective present_assignment) ∧

  (population  : Set ℝ) -- The set of all service lives
  (sample  : Set ℝ) -- The set of selected service lives
  (h_sample_subset  : sample ⊆ population) -- Sample is a subset of population
  (h_sample_size  : sample.ncard = 30) -- Sample size is 30
  (h_population_size  : population.ncard > 30) -- Population size is greater than 30
  (h_nonempty  : population.Nonempty) -- Population is nonempty
   : -- All statements are correct
  (-- Statement 1 : Population refers to all service lives
   (∃ p  : Set ℝ, p = population) ∧
   -- Statement 2 : Individual is service life of each tube
   (∀ x ∈ sample, x ∈ population) ∧
   -- Statement 3 : Sample is service lives of selected tubes
   (∃ s  : Set ℝ, s = sample) ∧
   -- Statement 4 : Sample size is 30
   (sample.ncard = 30)) := by

theorem solution_satisfies_system_10 (t  : ℝ) (C₁ C₂  : ℝ)  :
  let x  := fun t => 2*C₁*exp (2*t) + C₂*exp (3*t) - (3/2)*exp t + 2*t*exp (2*t)
  let y  := fun t => -C₁*exp (2*t) - C₂*exp (3*t) + (1/2)*exp t - (t + 1)*exp (2*t)
  let dx  := deriv x
  let dy  := deriv y
  (∀ t, dx t = x t - 2*y t + exp t) ∧ 
  (∀ t, dy t = x t + 4*y t + exp (2*t)) := by

    -- First term is 1
    a 1 = 1 →
    -- Geometric sequence condition
    (∀ n  : ℕ, n ≥ 1 → a (n + 1) = a n * r) →
    -- Arithmetic sequence condition
    2 * a 2 - 4 * a 1 = a 3 - 2 * a 2 →
    -- Conclusion : sum of first 4 terms equals 15
    a 1 + a 2 + a 3 + a 4 = 15 := by

    (I_new  : ℝ) (hI_new  : I_new = 1.2 * I) -- new income

    1 ≤ p.1 ∧ p.1 < p.2 ∧ p.2 ≤ n)
  (h_distinct  : ∀ (p q  : ℕ × ℕ), p ∈ S → q ∈ S → 
    (p.1 = q.1 ∧ p.2 = q.2) ∨ (p.1 ≠ q.1 ∧ p.2 ≠ q.2))  :

d9f7fce4.theorem:
theorem document_retyping_reduction 
  (original_sheets  : ℕ  := 350)
  (lines_per_sheet  : ℕ  := 85)
  (chars_per_line  : ℕ  := 100)
  (new_lines_per_sheet  : ℕ  := 120)
  (new_chars_per_line  : ℕ  := 110)
  (h_sheets_pos  : original_sheets > 0)
  (h_lines_pos  : lines_per_sheet > 0)
  (h_chars_pos  : chars_per_line > 0)
  (h_new_lines_pos  : new_lines_per_sheet > 0)
  (h_new_chars_pos  : new_chars_per_line > 0)  :
  let original_chars  := original_sheets * lines_per_sheet * chars_per_line
  let new_chars_per_sheet  := new_lines_per_sheet * new_chars_per_line
  let new_sheets  := (original_chars + new_chars_per_sheet - 1) / new_chars_per_sheet
  let reduction_percent  := (original_sheets - new_sheets  : ℝ) / original_sheets * 100
  abs (reduction_percent - 35.43) < 0.01 := by

    (h₁  : ∑ i in Finset.range n.succ, k i = m)
    (h₂  : ∑ i in Finset.range n.succ, i * k i = n)  :
    let fact_coeff  := n.factorial
    let arrange_k  := ∏ i in Finset.range n.succ, (k i).factorial

theorem sequence_next_term_2  : 
  let seq  := [2, 5, 11, 20, 32, 47]
  let diff₁  := seq[1] - seq[0]
  let diff₂  := seq[2] - seq[1]
  let diff₃  := seq[3] - seq[2]
  let diff₄  := seq[4] - seq[3]
  let diff₅  := seq[5] - seq[4]
  -- If differences increase by 3
  (diff₂ - diff₁ = 3) → 
  (diff₃ - diff₂ = 3) → 
  (diff₄ - diff₃ = 3) → 
  (diff₅ - diff₄ = 3) → 
  -- Then the next term is 65
  seq[5] + (diff₅ + 3) = 65 := by

theorem symmetric_sequence_19_terms 
  -- Assume we have a sequence c of length 19
  (c  : Fin 19 → ℝ)
  -- Assume it's symmetric : c_i = c_{20-i} for all i
  (h_sym  : ∀ i  : Fin 19, c i = c (19 - i + 1))
  -- Assume terms 10-19 form geometric sequence with first term 1 and ratio 2
  (h_geom_first  : c 10 = 1)
  (h_geom_ratio  : ∀ i  : Fin 9, c (i + 11) = 2 * c (i + 10))  :
  -- Then c₁₉ = 2^9 and sum of all terms = 2^11 - 3
  (c 19 = 2^9) ∧ (∑ i  : Fin 19, c i = 2^11 - 3) := by

  let b  := √3 -- semi-minor axis
  let F₁  := (-2, 0) -- left focus
  let l  := fun x => x + 2 -- line equation y = x + 2 (45° angle)
  let ellipse  := fun x y => x^2/4 + y^2/3 = 1 -- ellipse equation
  let x₁x₂  := 4/7 -- product of x-coordinates
  let x₁_plus_x₂  := -16/7 -- sum of x-coordinates
  -- Then the length of the chord is 12√2/7

9544f390.theorem:
theorem tangent_line_value_6 (a  : ℝ)  : 
  (∃ (k  : ℝ), -- slope of the line
    let line  := fun x => k * (x - 1) -- line equation y = k(x-1)
    -- Conditions for line being tangent to y = x³
    ∃ (x₀  : ℝ), 
      (line x₀ = x₀^3) ∧ -- point lies on y = x³
      (k = 3 * x₀^2) ∧   -- slope equals derivative at x₀
    -- Line also tangent to y = ax² + (15/4)x - 9
    ∃ (x₁  : ℝ),
      (line x₁ = a * x₁^2 + (15/4) * x₁ - 9) ∧
      (k = 2 * a * x₁ + 15/4)) →
  a = -25/64 ∨ a = -1 := by

  (h_pos_a  : a > 0)
  (h_pos_b  : b > 0)
  (h_pos_c  : c > 0)
  (h_ecc  : c/a = Real.sqrt 6/3) -- eccentricity condition
  (h_dist  : a = Real.sqrt 3) -- distance condition
  (h_rel  : a = Real.sqrt (b^2 + c^2))  : -- fundamental ellipse relation
  let A  : ℝ × ℝ  := (0, 1) -- intersection point A
  let B  : ℝ × ℝ  := (-3/2, -1/2) -- intersection point B
  let dist  := Real.sqrt ((A.1 - B.1)^2 + (A.2 - B.2)^2) -- distance formula

cdc65258.theorem:
theorem mascot_sales_analysis
  -- Initial conditions for part 1
  (initial_sales  : ℝ  := 50000)
  (final_sales  : ℝ  := 72000)
  -- Initial conditions for part 2
  (cost_price  : ℝ  := 40)
  (initial_price  : ℝ  := 80)
  (base_sales  : ℝ  := 20)
  (sales_increase_rate  : ℝ  := 4)  -- 2 units per $0.5 = 4 units per $1
  (target_profit  : ℝ  := 1400)
  (h_positive  : initial_sales > 0)
   : ∃ (growth_rate price_reduction  : ℝ),
    -- Part 1 : Monthly growth rate conditions
    growth_rate = 0.2 ∧
    initial_sales * (1 + growth_rate)^2 = final_sales ∧
    -- Part 2 : Price reduction conditions
    price_reduction = 30 ∧
    (initial_price - price_reduction - cost_price) * 
    (base_sales + sales_increase_rate * price_reduction) = target_profit := by

theorem regular_square_pyramid_volume  : 
  let edge_length  : ℝ  := 2
  let base_area  : ℝ  := edge_length * edge_length -- area of square base
  let slant_height  : ℝ  := Real.sqrt (edge_length^2 - (edge_length/2)^2) -- height from base to midpoint of edge
  let height  : ℝ  := Real.sqrt (edge_length^2 - (edge_length/√2)^2) -- height from base to apex
  let volume  : ℝ  := (1/3) * base_area * height -- pyramid volume formula
  volume = 4 * Real.sqrt 2 / 3 := by

723971eb.theorem:
theorem parabola_chord_distance (k  : ℝ) (h_chord  : k = 1 ∨ k = -1)  : 
  let line_eq (x y  : ℝ)  := y = k * x + 1
  let parabola_eq (x y  : ℝ)  := x^2 = 4 * y
  let dist_to_origin (a b c  : ℝ)  := |c| / Real.sqrt (a^2 + b^2)
  -- The chord length is 8
  (∀ x₁ x₂, parabola_eq x₁ (k * x₁ + 1) → parabola_eq x₂ (k * x₂ + 1) → 
    x₁ ≠ x₂ → Real.sqrt (1 + k^2) * Real.sqrt ((x₁ + x₂)^2 - 4 * x₁ * x₂) = 8) →
  -- Then the distance from origin to the line is √2/2
  dist_to_origin 1 k (-1) = Real.sqrt 2 / 2 := by

d3c09c80.theorem:
theorem max_inscribed_triangle_area  :
  let rect_width  : ℝ  := 12
  let rect_height  : ℝ  := 15
  let max_area  := 369 * Real.sqrt 3 - 540
  ∀ (m n  : ℂ), 
    -- Points are within rectangle bounds
    0 ≤ m.re ∧ m.re ≤ rect_width ∧ 0 ≤ m.im ∧ m.im ≤ rect_height →
    0 ≤ n.re ∧ n.re ≤ rect_width ∧ 0 ≤ n.im ∧ n.im ≤ rect_height →
    -- Triangle is equilateral
    Complex.abs (m - 0) = Complex.abs (n - 0) ∧
    Complex.abs (n - m) = Complex.abs (m - 0) →
    -- Area is at most max_area
    Real.sqrt 3 / 4 * Complex.abs m ^ 2 ≤ max_area := by

  -- Count occurrences of 13
  let count_13  := (data.filter (· = 13)).length
  -- For any other number x
  ∀ x  : ℕ, x ≠ 13 →
  -- Count of 13 is greater than count of x (mode property)
  (count_13 > (data.filter (· = x)).length) ∧
  -- 13 is the median
  (let sorted  := [10, 11, 13, 13, 13, 15, 16, 18, 21]
   13 = sorted[4]) := by

  (w  : ℕ → ℂ)
  (h₀  : ∀ n, w n ≠ w (n + 1))
  (h₁  : ∀ n, w n ∈ {z | z^1992 = k})

e0e0960d.theorem:
theorem floor_equation_solution_sum  : 
  let x₁  := -3/4
  let x₂  := -5/4
  -- Define the property of being a solution
  let is_solution (x  : ℝ)  := ⌊3 * x + 1⌋ = 2 * x - 1/2
  -- x₁ and x₂ are solutions
  (is_solution x₁) ∧ (is_solution x₂) ∧
  -- They are the only solutions
  (∀ x  : ℝ, is_solution x → x = x₁ ∨ x = x₂) ∧
  -- Their sum is -2
  x₁ + x₂ = -2 := by

3c8f405e.theorem:
theorem square_perimeter_segment_existence
  (Q  : Set ℝ × Set ℝ) -- The larger square
  (R  : Set ℝ × Set ℝ) -- The smaller square
  (hQ  : Q.1 = Set.Icc 0 500 ∧ Q.2 = Set.Icc 0 500) -- Q is a 500x500 square
  (hR  : R.1 = Set.Icc 125 375 ∧ R.2 = Set.Icc 125 375) -- R is a 250x250 square centered at (250,250)
   : ∃ (A B  : ℝ × ℝ), 
    -- A and B lie on perimeter of Q
    ((A.1 ∈ Q.1 ∧ A.2 ∈ Q.2) ∨ (A.1 ∈ Q.1 ∧ A.2 ∈ Q.2) ∨ 
     (A.1 ∈ Q.1 ∧ A.2 ∈ Q.2) ∨ (A.1 ∈ Q.1 ∧ A.2 ∈ Q.2)) ∧
    ((B.1 ∈ Q.1 ∧ B.2 ∈ Q.2) ∨ (B.1 ∈ Q.1 ∧ B.2 ∈ Q.2) ∨ 
     (B.1 ∈ Q.1 ∧ B.2 ∈ Q.2) ∨ (B.1 ∈ Q.1 ∧ B.2 ∈ Q.2)) ∧
    -- Segment AB doesn't intersect R
    (∀ P  : ℝ × ℝ, P ∈ (Set.Icc A.1 B.1 ×ˢ Set.Icc A.2 B.2) → 
      ¬(P.1 ∈ R.1 ∧ P.2 ∈ R.2)) ∧
    -- Length of AB is greater than 521
    Real.sqrt ((B.1 - A.1)^2 + (B.2 - A.2)^2) > 521 := by

b498e5b1.theorem:
theorem movie_marathon_duration  : 
  let movie1  := 2 -- First movie duration
  let movie2  := movie1 * 1.5 -- Second movie is 50% longer
  let movie3  := (movie1 + movie2) * 0.8 -- Third movie is 20% shorter
  let movie4  := movie2 * 2 -- Fourth movie is twice second
  let movie5  := movie3 - 0.5 -- Fifth movie is 30 min shorter than third
  let movie6  := (movie2 + movie4) / 2 -- Sixth movie is average
  let movie7  := 45 / movie5 -- Seventh movie inverse proportional
  movie1 + movie2 + movie3 + movie4 + movie5 + movie6 + movie7 = 35.857142857142857 := by

  (a b c  : ℝ) -- sides of triangle
  (h_pos_a  : 0 < a) (h_pos_b  : 0 < b) (h_pos_c  : 0 < c) -- positive sides
  (h_triangle  : a < b + c ∧ b < a + c ∧ c < a + b) -- triangle inequality
  (R₁ R₂  : ℝ) -- radii of circles
  (h_pos_R₁  : 0 < R₁) (h_pos_R₂  : 0 < R₂) -- positive radii
  (p  : ℝ) -- semi-perimeter
  (h_p  : p = (a + b + c) / 2) -- definition of semi-perimeter
   : let x_A  := (R₂ * (b + c)) / (R₁ + R₂) -- x-coordinate of A
    let x_P  := (R₂ * (b + c)) / (R₁ + R₂) -- x-coordinate of P
    x_A = x_P -- conclusion : A and P have same x-coordinate := by

theorem raft_drift_time (D a b  : ℝ) (hD  : D > 0) (ha  : a > 0) (hb  : b > 0) (hab  : b > a)  :
  let v_down  := D/a  -- downstream speed
  let v_up  := D/b    -- upstream speed
  let v_boat  := (v_down + v_up)/2  -- boat speed in still water
  let v_current  := (v_down - v_up)/2  -- current speed
  let raft_time  := D/v_current  -- time for raft
  raft_time = 2*a*b/(b-a) := by

  (x_sum y_sum  : ℝ)
  (x_var_sum y_var_sum  : ℝ)
  (cov_sum  : ℝ)
  (hn  : n = 20)
  (hN  : N = 200)
  (hx_sum  : x_sum = 60)
  (hy_sum  : y_sum = 1200)
  (hx_var  : x_var_sum = 80)
  (hy_var  : y_var_sum = 9000)
  (hcov  : cov_sum = 800)
   : let x_mean  := x_sum / n
    let y_mean  := y_sum / n
    let x_var  := x_var_sum / n
    let y_var  := y_var_sum / n
    let cov  := cov_sum / n
    let r  := cov / (Real.sqrt (x_var * y_var))
    let pop_est  := y_mean * N
    (r = 2 * Real.sqrt 2 / 3 ∧ pop_est = 12000) := by

    (right_at_A  : (B.1 - A.1) * (C.1 - A.1) + (B.2 - A.2) * (C.2 - A.2) = 0)
    (right_at_C  : (B.1 - C.1) * (D.1 - C.1) + (B.2 - C.2) * (D.2 - C.2) = 0)
    (on_AC  : (B.1 - A.1) * (D.2 - C.2) = (B.2 - A.2) * (D.1 - C.1)) -- B,D on same line as AC
    (A_ne_C  : (C.1 - A.1)^2 + (C.2 - A.2)^2 ≠ 0) -- AC is not zero
    (AB₁ CD₁  : ℝ) -- projections
    (h_AB₁  : AB₁ = (B.1 - A.1) * (C.1 - A.1) + (B.2 - A.2) * (C.2 - A.2)) -- projection formula
    (h_CD₁  : CD₁ = (D.1 - C.1) * (C.1 - A.1) + (D.2 - C.2) * (C.2 - A.2)) -- projection formula
     : AB₁ = CD₁ := by

  -- FG is a cumulative distribution function
  (-- FG is non-decreasing
   (∀ x y, x ≤ y → FG x ≤ FG y) ∧ 
   -- FG approaches 0 as x approaches -∞
   (∀ ε > 0, ∃ M, ∀ x, x ≤ M → |FG x| < ε) ∧

   (∀ ε > 0, ∃ M, ∀ x, x ≥ M → |FG x - 1| < ε)) := by

  (A B C D M  : ℝ × ℝ) -- Points representing chord endpoints and intersection
  (hr₁  : r₁ = 4) -- Radius of smaller circle
  (hr₂  : r₂ = 6) -- Radius of larger circle
  (hM  : M = ((A.1 + B.1)/2, (A.2 + B.2)/2)) -- M is midpoint of AB
  (hAB  : dist A B = 2) -- Length of AB is 2
  (hO₁  : dist O A = r₁ ∧ dist O B = r₁) -- A and B lie on first circle
  (hO₂  : dist O C = r₂ ∧ dist O D = r₂) -- C and D lie on second circle
  (hLine  : ∃ t₁ t₂  : ℝ, C = (1-t₁) • A + t₁ • B ∧ D = (1-t₂) • A + t₂ • B) -- C and D lie on AB
